title: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: The weeknight classic. Slow-simmered mince, rich tinned tomato, a knob of butter to finish.

steps.content: Dice onion, mince garlic.
steps.detail: Halve and peel the onion, keeping the root on to stop your eyes watering. Make lengthwise cuts, then across — aim for small, even dice (about 5mm). Smash garlic with the flat of a knife to loosen the skin, then mince or grate. Having everything prepped before you start the heat is called mise en place — makes the cook relaxed, not frantic.

steps.content: Brown mince in olive oil, 6 min.
steps.detail: Heat a glug of oil in a heavy pan over medium-high until it shimmers. Crumble the mince in in large chunks — don't break it up straight away. Let it sit untouched for 2 min to deep-brown on the bottom. Then start breaking up with a wooden spoon as you stir. That browning is flavour. Cook until no pink remains.

steps.content: Add onion and garlic, cook until soft.
steps.detail: Drop heat to medium. Tip in onion and garlic — they'll sizzle and soften in the mince fat. Cook 5 min, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent and starting to gold at the edges. If the pan looks dry, a splash of water helps. Season with a big pinch of salt now.

steps.content: Tip in tomatoes, simmer 20 min.
steps.detail: Crush whole tomatoes with your hand as you pour them in. Add a pinch of salt, a crack of pepper, and a knob of butter if you've got one — it rounds off the acid. Simmer uncovered on low — big lazy bubbles, not a rolling boil — for 20 min, stirring every few minutes. It should reduce to a rich, glossy sauce that coats a spoon.

steps.content: Cook spaghetti.
steps.detail: Bring a big pot of water to a rolling boil. Salt it heavily — it should taste like sea water. Add spaghetti, stir to prevent clumping. Cook 1 min less than the packet time for al dente. Before draining, scoop out a cup of pasta water — the starchy water emulsifies the sauce and pasta.

steps.content: Toss through sauce, top with parmesan.
steps.detail: Drag the pasta straight from the pot into the sauce with tongs — a little water clinging is fine. Toss for a minute over low heat. If the sauce looks tight, splash in pasta water. Parmesan grated over the top, crack of pepper, eat immediately while it's still steaming.

title: Green Thai Curry
description: Bright, punchy green curry with chicken thigh and coconut milk.

steps.content: Fry curry paste in a splash of oil, 1 min.
steps.detail: Heat a tablespoon of oil in a wide pan over medium. Spoon in the curry paste and stir constantly for a full minute — you'll see it go from matte to shiny, smell it bloom. This step wakes the paste up; skip it and the curry stays flat. If it starts to catch, drop the heat.

steps.content: Add diced chicken, toss to coat.
steps.detail: Cut thigh into bite-sized pieces (about 3cm — bigger than you think, they shrink). Tip into the paste and stir until every piece wears a green coat. Cook 2 min to just start colouring the edges — not fully cooking through yet.

steps.content: Pour in coconut milk, simmer 12 min.
steps.detail: Shake the tin first — the cream and water often separate. Pour it all in, including any solid cream on top. Stir to dissolve the paste into the coconut milk. Bring to a bare simmer (small bubbles) and cook 10–12 min until the chicken is cooked and the sauce has tightened slightly. Don't boil hard — it splits coconut milk.

steps.content: Season with fish sauce and lime.
steps.detail: Off heat for tasting (flavours are less bold hot). Stir in fish sauce, then squeeze half the lime. Taste. It should be balanced — salty from fish sauce, sour from lime, a touch sweet from the coconut. Adjust in tiny increments. More lime if flat, more fish sauce if too sweet.

steps.content: Over rice, basil and chilli.
steps.detail: Pile over jasmine rice. Tear basil (don't chop — basil bruises, turns dark). Sliced fresh chilli on top for heat. Extra lime wedges on the side. A spoon is better than a fork — you want broth with every bite.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Yoghurt-marinated chicken in a tomato-cream sauce. Better than takeaway.

steps.content: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and curry powder.
steps.detail: Cube chicken thighs into 3cm pieces. In a bowl, mix with yoghurt, curry powder, a big pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon if you've got one. Yoghurt tenderises the meat — its lactic acid breaks down fibres gently. 30 min is good; overnight is transformative. Cover and fridge.

steps.content: Sear chicken in hot pan, set aside.
steps.detail: Heat a splash of oil in a heavy pan until shimmering. Shake excess marinade off each piece and sear in batches — overcrowding steams. 2 min a side for deep gold char patches. Don't cook through yet; it finishes in the sauce. Remove to a plate.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic.
steps.detail: Same pan — don't wash, those fond bits are flavour. Lower to medium. Finely chopped onion, a splash of oil if dry. 6 min till soft and golden at the edges. Add grated garlic the last minute.

steps.content: Add tomatoes, simmer 10 min, then cream.
steps.detail: Tip in tomatoes (crush with your hand). Stir, season, simmer 10 min uncovered till thick and the raw tomato edge is gone. Off heat, stir in cream — don't let it boil hard once cream's in, it can split.

steps.content: Return chicken, simmer 8 min.
steps.detail: Chicken back with any resting juices. Simmer gently 8 min till cooked through (juices run clear when pierced). Taste — salt, a pinch of sugar if too sharp.

steps.content: Over basmati, scatter coriander.
steps.detail: Serve over fluffy basmati rice. Tear coriander over the top — a lot of it. A dollop of extra yoghurt on the side is lovely. Warm naan if you have it.

title: Haloumi Grain Bowl
description: Crispy haloumi, roast pumpkin, rocket, lemon. Lunch goals.

steps.content: Roast pumpkin with oil and cumin, 25 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: Chunk pumpkin into 3cm cubes, skin on or off. Toss on a tray with a glug of olive oil, a teaspoon of cumin, flakes of salt, a grind of pepper. Spread in a single layer with space between pieces — crowded veg steams instead of caramelising. 25 min at 200°C, give the tray a shake halfway. Done when edges are deep gold and a knife slides through easily.

steps.content: Cook rice.
steps.detail: One cup basmati, rinsed under cold water till the water runs clear — that washes off surface starch and gives fluffier grains. Into a pot with 1.5 cups water, pinch of salt, lid on. Bring to a boil, drop to the lowest flame, 12 min. Don't lift the lid. Off heat, still covered, 5 min. Fork through to separate.

steps.content: Fry haloumi slices till golden both sides.
steps.detail: Slice haloumi into 1cm slabs — thinner goes rubbery, thicker won't get a crust. Pat each piece dry with paper towel; surface moisture prevents browning. Dry pan over medium-high, no oil (haloumi is already rich). Lay slices in and don't touch for 2 min — let the crust form. Flip once. Eat within a few minutes; haloumi squeaks on the tooth when it cools.

steps.content: Assemble bowl: rice, rocket, pumpkin, haloumi.
steps.detail: Wide shallow bowls work better than deep ones — spread the beauty out. Warm rice as the base, big handfuls of rocket tumbled on one side, pumpkin clumped on another, haloumi slabs leaning together. Let colours sit next to each other rather than tossing — you eat with your eyes first.

steps.content: Squeeze lemon over the lot.
steps.detail: Halve the lemon and squeeze firmly over everything — cut-side-up stops pips dropping. The acid lifts the pumpkin's sweetness and cuts through haloumi's salt. Another drizzle of olive oil, crack of pepper. If you have dukkah or toasted seeds, scatter those too. Eat warm or room temp — this bowl doesn't mind either.

title: Sausage Pasta Bake
description: Snags out of their skins, tomato, pasta, a crust of cheese. Feeds a crowd.

steps.content: Squeeze snag meat out of skins, brown in pan.
steps.detail: Slit each snag lengthways with a small knife, peel the skin off like a banana. The meat inside is your mince, free of charge, already seasoned. Heat a glug of oil in a big heavy pan over medium-high. Break the sausage meat in with your fingers in rough chunks. Let sit 2 min for colour, then break up with a wooden spoon. Cook till no pink remains and some edges have browned, 6 min.

steps.content: Add diced onion and garlic, soften.
steps.detail: Lower heat to medium. Finely dice a brown onion — aim for small, even pieces so they melt into the sauce. Tip in with grated or crushed garlic. Everything sizzles in the sausage fat (flavour!). Stir 5 min till onion is translucent and softening. A pinch of salt now helps draw moisture out.

steps.content: Pour in tomatoes, simmer.
steps.detail: Pour in two tins of tomatoes, crushing whole ones with your hand as you go. Splash some water into each tin to rinse out the rest and tip that in too. Salt, pepper, pinch of sugar if the tomatoes taste sharp. Bring to a bare bubble, drop the heat, 10 min uncovered till the sauce thickens and the raw tomato edge is gone.

steps.content: Cook pasta, reserving some water.
steps.detail: Big pot, heavily salted water (taste like the sea), rolling boil. Cook pasta 2 min LESS than the packet says — it'll finish cooking in the oven and you don't want mush. Before draining, scoop out a cup of pasta water. That starchy water loosens the sauce later if it tightens up.

steps.content: Combine, tip into dish, top with mozzarella.
steps.detail: Fold the pasta through the sauce with a big splash of reserved water — it should look a little loose; it'll drink up more in the oven. Tip into a baking dish. Tear mozzarella into chunks and dot evenly across the top — don't carpet it flat, you want molten pockets and crispy edges.

steps.content: Bake 15 min at 200°C until bubbly.
steps.detail: 220°C oven for 15 min till the top is bubbling and blistered with gold patches. If it's looking pale, flash under the grill for 90 seconds — watch it like a hawk, cheese goes from gold to burnt in seconds. Rest 5 min before scooping — the sauce sets up and stops being molten. Tear basil over the top.

title: Sunday Roast Chook
description: Whole chicken, crispy skin, lemon and garlic.

steps.content: Preheat oven to 200°C.
steps.detail: Get the oven going first thing — by the time you've prepped, it's ready. A roast needs high heat. Pull the chicken out of the fridge when you turn the oven on — cold meat cooks unevenly.

steps.content: Pat chook dry, rub with oil and salt.
steps.detail: Paper towels inside and out — dry skin = crispy skin. Rub all over with olive oil, then coat heavily with sea salt and cracked pepper. Don't skimp on seasoning — a 1.6kg bird can take a good tablespoon of salt spread around. Massage into the legs, under the wings, everywhere.

steps.content: Stuff cavity with lemon and garlic.
steps.detail: Halve a lemon and a whole garlic head across the equator. Stuff inside the cavity — they steam the bird from inside and flavour the juices. A few thyme sprigs if you have them. Tying the legs with string keeps the thighs juicy but it's optional.

steps.content: Roast on potatoes, 75 min.
steps.detail: Chunk potatoes (skin on), toss in oil and salt. Spread in a roasting tray, sit the chicken on top breast-side-up. The chicken fat drips onto the potatoes — best roast potatoes in the world. 75 min for a 1.6kg bird: juices run clear when pierced in the thigh, and a thermometer reads 75°C in the thickest part. Baste halfway with pan juices if you remember.

steps.content: Rest 10 min before carving.
steps.detail: Non-negotiable. Lift the chicken onto a board, tent loosely with foil. The juices that would run out onto the board now redistribute into the meat. Use the 10 min to stir pan juices into a quick gravy if you like — a splash of stock, boil hard, done.

title: Veggie Fried Rice
description: Leftover rice, a fridge raid, one pan. Dinner in 10.

steps.content: Scramble eggs, set aside.
steps.detail: Crack two eggs into a bowl and beat with a splash of soy if you're organised. Hot wok or wide pan, small glug of oil. Pour eggs in — they should sizzle on contact. Scramble with a spatula in big folds for 30 sec till just set, still glossy. They'll finish cooking from residual heat. Tip onto a plate.

steps.content: Fry garlic and diced carrot.
steps.detail: Same pan (don't wash it), more oil if needed. Drop heat to medium. Grated garlic for 20 sec till fragrant — not brown. Small-diced carrot goes in (2mm cubes cook fast). 2 min stirring till the carrot just softens at the edges but keeps a little bite.

steps.content: Add peas and cold rice, toss over high heat.
steps.detail: Now crank the heat to max. Tip in frozen peas and the cold leftover rice — cold rice is KEY. Fresh warm rice turns gluey when stir-fried. Use a spatula to break up rice clumps and toss everything till the rice is heated through and starting to crisp at the edges, 3 min. High heat, fast hands.

steps.content: Pour soy sauce down the side of the pan.
steps.detail: Instead of pouring soy onto the rice (it pools and seasons unevenly), tilt the pan and pour soy down the side — it hits the hot metal, sizzles and evaporates a bit, coats evenly as you toss. 2 tablespoons is plenty. This is the wok-hei trick.

steps.content: Fold in eggs and spring onion.
steps.detail: Break the scrambled eggs into bite-sized chunks with the spatula and fold back through with sliced spring onions. 30 sec to warm the eggs. Off heat, finish with a drizzle of sesame oil if you've got some. Eat straight from the pan; fried rice gets claggy if it waits.

title: Pumpkin & Chickpea Curry
description: Sweet pumpkin, creamy coconut, warming curry powder.

steps.content: Soften onion with curry powder.
steps.detail: Finely dice a brown onion. Heat a glug of oil in a heavy pan over medium. Add onion with a pinch of salt — salt pulls moisture out and speeds softening. Cook 6 min till translucent. Sprinkle in 2 tablespoons of curry powder and stir constantly for 30 sec to wake the spices up and coat the onion. It'll smell incredible — that's the signal to move on.

steps.content: Add diced pumpkin, toss to coat.
steps.detail: Cube pumpkin into 2.5cm chunks, skin off (or on if you don't mind — it softens in the coconut milk). Tip into the spiced onions and toss till every cube wears an orange jacket. 2 min to start the edges softening. This builds the pumpkin's flavour before the liquid goes in.

steps.content: Pour in coconut milk, simmer 20 min.
steps.detail: Shake the tin of coconut milk first (cream and water separate). Pour it all in. Splash half a cup of water into the empty tin, swirl, tip in too. Bring to a bare simmer — small lazy bubbles, not a rolling boil, which would split the coconut milk. Lid on slightly ajar, 18 min till pumpkin is fork-tender.

steps.content: Add chickpeas and spinach for last 3 min.
steps.detail: Drain and rinse the chickpeas (the tin brine can taste metallic). Stir in with two handfuls of spinach for the last 3 min. Spinach wilts almost instantly; chickpeas just need warming through. If the sauce looks tight, loosen with a splash of water. Taste — salt, maybe a squeeze of lime to lift it.

steps.content: Serve over basmati.
steps.detail: Fluff basmati with a fork. Ladle curry into shallow bowls, rice alongside (not under — it goes mushy). A dollop of yoghurt cools it if the spice has kicked up, torn coriander on top. Pappadums or flatbread if you've got them — curry wants something to scoop with.

title: Lemon Chicken & Rice
description: One-pan, lemon-bright, weeknight winner.

steps.content: Sear chicken in oil, skin-side down.
steps.detail: Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towel — wet skin won't crisp. Season both sides well with salt and pepper. Heat a glug of oil in a heavy oven-safe pan over medium-high. Lay thighs in SKIN-SIDE DOWN and don't touch them for 5 min — let the skin render its fat and turn mahogany. Flip for 1 min to seal the other side.

steps.content: Remove, add garlic and rice, toast 1 min.
steps.detail: Lift chicken out to a plate (it's not cooked through yet, that's fine — finishes later). Leave the golden fat in the pan. Drop heat to medium. Add four garlic cloves, thinly sliced — they should sizzle but not burn. Tip in rinsed rice and stir 60 sec till every grain is glossed with chickeny fat.

steps.content: Pour in stock and lemon juice.
steps.detail: Pour in 3 cups of chicken stock and the juice of a whole lemon. Scrape the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon to lift any browned bits — that's your flavour. Season with salt and pepper, bring to a boil.

steps.content: Return chicken, cover, simmer 22 min.
steps.detail: Nestle the chicken thighs skin-side up on top of the rice — they'll poke out above the liquid, which keeps the skin crispy while the rice cooks below. Lid on tight, drop to the lowest flame. 22 min. Don't lift the lid — every peek lets steam escape and the rice ends up unevenly cooked.

steps.content: Rest 5 min, scatter parsley.
steps.detail: Off heat, lid still on, 5 min. Critical rest — the rice absorbs the last of the stock and the chicken juices redistribute. Scatter with roughly chopped parsley and serve straight from the pan. Lemon wedges on the side for anyone who wants more zing.

title: BBQ Snag Sanga
description: Bread, snag, grilled onions, sauce. A national treasure.

steps.content: Fire up the BBQ.
steps.detail: Gas BBQ: turn all burners to medium-high, lid down, 10 min. You want the grills hot enough that a drop of water hisses off instantly. Scrub the grills clean with a wire brush while it heats. Charcoal BBQ: light coals 30 min ahead; ready when they're glowing orange with a light grey dust on top. Oil the grills lightly with a tongs-held paper towel dipped in oil.

steps.content: Slice onions, fry in butter until golden.
steps.detail: Halve the onions and slice them as thin as you dare — they cook down a LOT. Knob of butter in a pan on the BBQ side burner (or a pan off to the side of a coal BBQ). Medium-low heat. Cook 20 min, stirring every few minutes, till deeply golden and sweet. A pinch of salt helps, and don't rush — patient onions are the heart of a good sanga.

steps.content: Grill snags, turning often.
steps.detail: Snags on the grill, lid down for gas or cover slightly for coal. Turn every 2 min — this stops them splitting and gives even colour all round. 12 min total till firm to the squeeze and deep gold. Don't prick them; juices that run out are flavour that's lost. If flare-ups happen, move them to a cooler spot.

steps.content: Butter bread, fold around snag and onions.
steps.detail: Slice of white bread per snag. Smear with butter (it stops the bread going soggy from sausage fat). Snag laid diagonally across (corner-to-corner is the classic Bunnings fold), pile of onions on top, fold bread over into a triangle. Tomato sauce drawn on in a spiral is the traditional move. Eat standing up.

title: Prawn Pad Thai
description: Rice noodles, prawns, peanuts, lime. Takeaway at home.

steps.content: Mix fish sauce, peanut butter, lime for sauce.
steps.detail: In a small bowl, whisk 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tbsp peanut butter, juice of half a lime, 1 tsp sugar or honey. Loosen with a splash of hot water till it pours easily. Taste — should hit salty, sour, sweet, a touch nutty. Adjust in tiny increments. Pre-mixing means you're not scrambling once the wok is hot.

steps.content: Fry prawns 2 min, remove.
steps.detail: Pat prawns dry. Hot pan or wok, splash of oil. Prawns in a single layer, don't crowd — they steam instead of sear if piled up. 60 sec one side till pink and curling, flip, 60 sec the other side. That's it — any longer and they're rubbery. Lift onto a plate.

steps.content: Scramble eggs in pan.
steps.detail: Same pan, drop heat to medium. Crack 2 eggs straight in. Let them sit 20 sec, then scramble with a spatula in big soft folds till just set. Push to one side of the pan. Fast — you don't want dry egg.

steps.content: Toss cooked rice noodles with sauce (use spaghetti in a pinch).
steps.detail: Rice noodles should be soaked per packet (usually 8 min in hot water till pliable but still firm). Drain. Tip into the pan with the sauce. Use tongs to toss everything — noodles soak up sauce fast. 60 sec till glossy and coated. If it looks dry, splash of water. Spaghetti pre-cooked 1 min short of packet works surprisingly well in a pinch.

steps.content: Return prawns, top with spring onion and chilli.
steps.detail: Prawns back in with resting juices. Toss 20 sec to warm through. Off heat. Thin-sliced spring onions, fresh chilli, torn coriander, chopped peanuts over the top. Extra lime wedge on the side. Eat immediately, chopsticks or fork — whatever works.

title: Smashed Avo on Toast
description: The brunch standard. Sourdough, avo, feta, lemon, chilli flakes.

steps.content: Toast bread.
steps.detail: Thick-cut sourdough if you have it. Toast well — you want a sturdy base that holds avo without going soggy. Under the grill toasts more evenly than a pop-up. One side, flip, watch for deep gold.

steps.content: Mash avo with lemon and salt.
steps.detail: Ripe avo gives easily when you press near the stem. Halve, twist apart, lift out the stone. Scoop flesh into a bowl. Squeeze lemon and season with salt flakes — add lemon BEFORE mashing so it doesn't brown. Mash with a fork — chunky, not puréed. Texture is good.

steps.content: Pile on toast, crumble feta.
steps.detail: Spread avo generously, right to the edges. Feta crumbled between your fingers (more rustic than cubed). Don't hold back on the feta — it balances the richness.

steps.content: Drizzle oil, chilli flakes.
steps.detail: Good olive oil in a proper drizzle. Chilli flakes or fresh chilli if you've got it. A final crack of salt on top. Eat immediately — avo toast waits for no one.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Soft tortillas, spiced mince, avo, lime.

steps.content: Brown mince with cumin and paprika.
steps.detail: Hot pan, splash of oil. Crumble mince in and let it sit 2 min for a deep brown crust before breaking up. Add cumin, paprika, salt, pepper; stir through. Cook 6 min till no pink remains. A splash of water at the end loosens it into tacos-friendly texture.

steps.content: Dice tomato, avo. Squeeze lime.
steps.detail: Small dice on the tomato — drain on paper towel for 2 min if juicy so tacos don't get soggy. Halve and scoop avo, chop rough. Have a bowl of lime wedges, chopped coriander, hot sauce ready.

steps.content: Warm tortillas.
steps.detail: Small, soft corn tortillas are traditional but flour is fine. Warm each directly on a gas flame or dry pan for 15 sec per side till pliable and a few char spots. Keep warm wrapped in a clean tea towel — they go stiff fast.

steps.content: Build at the table.
steps.detail: Lay out components and let everyone build their own. Tortilla, mince, tomato, avo, coriander, hot sauce, big squeeze of lime. Two tortillas stacked per taco is the Mexico City trick — the second catches drippings. Eat over a plate.

title: Zucchini Slice
description: The lunchbox legend. Eggs, zucchini, bacon, a bit of cheese.

steps.content: Grate zucchini, squeeze out moisture.
steps.detail: Coarse-grater on the biggest holes. Grate the zucchini into a clean tea towel or a colander over a bowl. Sprinkle a big pinch of salt through, rest 5 min — the salt draws out water. Then wring HARD. This step is non-negotiable; skip it and your slice is a soggy puddle. You should pour off at least a cup of liquid. Wet zucchini is the enemy.

steps.content: Mix with beaten eggs, flour, diced bacon, onion, cheese.
steps.detail: Beat 5 eggs in a big bowl with a fork. Tip in the squeezed zucchini, 1 cup plain flour, 4 diced rashers of bacon (raw is fine, it'll cook through), one finely diced brown onion, and 100g grated cheddar. Salt, pepper. Stir with a wooden spoon till just combined — overmixing develops gluten and makes it tough.

steps.content: Pour into lined tin.
steps.detail: Line a 20x30cm slice tin with baking paper — grease first so the paper sticks. Pour the mix in and level with a spatula. It looks loose; it'll set as it cooks. Give the tin a gentle tap on the bench to release any air pockets.

steps.content: Bake 35 min at 180°C until set and golden.
steps.detail: 180°C oven, middle rack, 35 min. Done when the top is deep gold and a skewer in the centre comes out clean. The edges should be pulling away from the paper. If it's browning too fast, loosely tent with foil. Cool in the tin 10 min before lifting out — lets it set. Slice into squares; eats hot, warm, or fridge-cold for lunchboxes.

title: Chicken Caesar Salad
description: Crisp lettuce, smoky bacon, parmesan, a punchy dressing.

steps.content: Grill chicken, slice.
steps.detail: Pat breasts dry, season both sides with salt and pepper. If they're thick, butterfly them for even cooking. Hot pan, splash of oil, medium-high. 5 min a side without moving them — let the crust form. Internal temp 72°C in the thickest part. Rest on a board 5 min (juices settle back in), then slice across the grain into thick strips.

steps.content: Fry bacon until crisp, tear.
steps.detail: Lay rashers flat in a cold pan, no oil — bacon renders its own. Turn to medium. 6 min, flipping once, till deeply gold and crisp at the edges. Drain on paper towel. When cool enough to handle, tear into shards rather than chop — rustic texture wins.

steps.content: Toast bread, cube for croutons.
steps.detail: Same pan still has bacon fat. Cube bread into 2cm chunks and toss in the fat over medium. 4 min, tossing often, till golden and crunchy on all sides. Those are your croutons — infinitely better than shop-bought.

steps.content: Boil eggs 6 min, halve.
steps.detail: Lower eggs gently into simmering water on a spoon. Timer for EXACTLY 6 min — set white, still a bit custardy in the centre. Straight into iced water to stop the cook and loosen the shell. Peel under a gentle stream. Halve lengthways.

steps.content: Toss leaves with dressing, top with all.
steps.detail: Tear cos leaves into big bite-sized pieces into the biggest bowl you own. Dressing (anchovy, garlic, dijon, lemon, oil, parmesan, yolk) drizzled a little at a time, tossing with your hands till every leaf is barely coated. Don't drown it. Top with chicken, bacon shards, croutons, halved eggs, shaved parmesan. Pepper, lemon wedge.

title: Tuna Pasta
description: Pantry-raid dinner. Spaghetti, tuna, lemon, chilli.

steps.content: Cook pasta.
steps.detail: Big pot of heavily salted water — taste like the sea, that's your only pasta seasoning. Rolling boil, then spaghetti. Stir once to prevent clumping. Cook 1 min less than the packet for al dente. Before draining, scoop out a full cup of pasta water — that starchy liquid is what makes the sauce cling.

steps.content: Warm oil with garlic and chilli.
steps.detail: Wide pan, glug of good olive oil, two thinly sliced garlic cloves and a finely chopped chilli (or a big pinch of flakes). Medium-low heat — cold start is fine too. You want the garlic pale gold and fragrant, never brown. Brown garlic is bitter and will ruin the dish. 3 min gentle sizzle.

steps.content: Flake in tuna, lemon zest.
steps.detail: Off heat. Drain tuna well (oil or springwater, doesn't matter) and flake straight into the pan with a fork. Grate in the zest of half a lemon (not the white pith, bitter). Stir once — the residual heat warms the tuna without drying it out.

steps.content: Toss with pasta and parsley.
steps.detail: Drained pasta into the pan with a good splash of reserved water. Juice of half a lemon, torn parsley, toss hard with tongs for 60 sec — the water emulsifies with the oil into a glossy sauce. More water if it looks tight. Crack of pepper, eat immediately. No cheese traditionally — tuna doesn't want it.

title: Red Lentil Dahl
description: The humble hero. Creamy, spiced, cheap.

steps.content: Soften onion, garlic, ginger.
steps.detail: Finely dice a brown onion, grate 3 garlic cloves and a thumb of ginger. Heat 2 tbsp oil (or ghee if you've got it) in a heavy pan over medium. Onion in with a pinch of salt, cook 6 min till translucent and starting to gold. Add garlic and ginger for the last minute — they burn quicker than onion.

steps.content: Add spices, toast 1 min.
steps.detail: Sprinkle in 2 tbsp curry powder (or a mix of turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala if you're spice-stocked). Stir constantly for 60 sec in the hot oil — blooming wakes up the spices and releases their essential oils. You'll smell the difference. Don't walk away; spices burn in seconds.

steps.content: Tip in lentils, tomato, coconut milk, 3 cups water.
steps.detail: Rinse 1.5 cups of red lentils under cold water till it runs clearer — washes off starch and dust. Tip into the pan with a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand), 200ml coconut milk, and 3 cups of water. Stir, scraping up any spice stuck to the pan — that's all flavour.

steps.content: Simmer 25 min until thick.
steps.detail: Bring to a bare simmer, drop heat low. 25 min uncovered, stirring every 5 min so the lentils don't catch on the bottom. They'll break down into a creamy porridge consistency. If it gets too thick, splash of water. Too thin, simmer longer. Season well at the end — lentils need more salt than you think.

steps.content: Serve over rice.
steps.detail: Fluff basmati. Ladle dahl into bowls, rice on the side. A swirl of yoghurt or extra coconut milk on top looks the part. Tear coriander over. If you have time, fry a sliced garlic clove in ghee till golden with a pinch of chilli flakes and spoon that over — that's tarka, the traditional finish, and it transforms the bowl.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Slow-stirred, silky, generous with parmesan.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic in butter.
steps.detail: Finely dice a brown onion and grate 2 garlic cloves. Melt a good knob of butter in a heavy wide pan over medium. Onion in, pinch of salt, 8 min till soft and translucent — don't colour, this isn't fry-brown territory, we want sweetness. Garlic for the last minute.

steps.content: Add rice, toast 2 min.
steps.detail: Tip in 1.5 cups of arborio or short-grain rice (jasmine will do in a pinch but it's less traditional). Stir 2 min till every grain is glossy with butter and the edges turn slightly translucent — that's toasting. Toasted rice won't go gluey and will hold its bite.

steps.content: Splash in wine, stir till absorbed.
steps.detail: Crank heat to medium-high. Pour in half a cup of white wine — it'll hiss and bubble hard. Keep stirring till it's almost fully absorbed and the alcohol has cooked off (smells winey, not sharp). 2 min. This is the flavour backbone.

steps.content: Add stock a ladle at a time, stirring, 20 min.
steps.detail: Warm the stock in a pot nearby — cold stock shocks the rice. Ladle by ladle: pour in enough to just cover the rice, stir continuously till mostly absorbed, then another. Repeat for 18–20 min. The stirring releases starch that makes risotto creamy. Taste at 18 min — rice should be tender with a tiny bite at the centre.

steps.content: Fold in fried mushrooms and parmesan.
steps.detail: Off heat. Fold in a handful of mushrooms that you've separately fried hard in butter till deep gold (mushrooms need high heat alone, else they go grey and limp). Add 50g grated parmesan and another knob of butter — that's the mantecatura, the silk-making finish. Cover, rest 2 min. It should spread slowly like lava on the plate, not stand up stiff.

title: Fish Tacos
description: Flaky barramundi, cabbage slaw, lime crema.

steps.content: Season fish with paprika, pan-fry 3 min each side.
steps.detail: Pat barramundi fillets dry with paper towel — wet fish won't form a crust. Dust both sides with smoked paprika, salt, pepper. Hot pan with a splash of oil, medium-high. Fillets in and don't touch for 3 min — let the spice crust form. Gentle flip with a fish spatula, another 3 min. Fish is done when it flakes easily with a fork and the flesh is opaque.

steps.content: Shred lettuce.
steps.detail: Core the lettuce and slice thinly across the grain — ribbons work better than chunks in a taco. Iceberg or cos both work. Keep crisp in iced water if you're prepping ahead, then spin dry.

steps.content: Stir yoghurt with lime juice for crema.
steps.detail: In a bowl, combine half a cup of yoghurt with the juice of one lime, a pinch of salt, a crushed garlic clove if you want more kick. Whisk till smooth. This is your crema — cooling, tangy, cuts through the rich fish.

steps.content: Flake fish, fill warm tortillas, top with slaw and crema.
steps.detail: Warm tortillas on a dry pan or direct gas flame for 15 sec a side — you want pliable, with a few char spots. Keep them wrapped in a clean tea towel so they don't stiffen. Flake the fish into chunks with a fork. Pile fish in each tortilla, shredded lettuce, generous spoon of crema, a squeeze of extra lime, torn coriander. Two tortillas stacked holds messy fillings.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato sauce. Weekend brunch.

steps.content: Soften onion, capsicum, garlic.
steps.detail: Slice onion and capsicum thinly. Cast iron or a heavy pan that can go straight to the table is ideal. Warm a glug of oil over medium, add onion and capsicum, cook 8 min till truly soft and starting to catch at the edges. Garlic (grated or minced) the last minute — burnt garlic ruins everything.

steps.content: Add paprika, then tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Stir paprika into the hot veg for 30 seconds to bloom its flavour. Tip in tomatoes, crush with a spoon. Season with salt, a pinch of sugar, cracked pepper. Simmer on low for 10 min till thick enough that a spoon leaves a trail through it.

steps.content: Make wells, crack eggs into them.
steps.detail: Use the back of a spoon to make little wells in the sauce — as many as eggs you have. Crack each egg into a small bowl first (so you can fish out any shell), then slide into its well. This lets the whites sit IN the sauce, not float on top.

steps.content: Cover, cook 5 min till whites set.
steps.detail: Lid on, low heat. 5 min for whites just-set with runny yolks — the goal. Check at 4 min — they keep cooking off the heat. If your pan has no lid, a large upside-down plate or foil works.

steps.content: Crumble feta, serve with bread.
steps.detail: Dot feta between eggs — it softens in the warmth. Scatter parsley if you have it. Serve straight from the pan with crusty bread for scooping. This is finger food with cutlery.

title: Pesto Pasta
description: Fresh basil, parmesan, garlic. Fifteen-minute dinner.

steps.content: Blitz basil, garlic, parmesan, oil, lemon.
steps.detail: Into a food processor (or mortar and pestle for the purists): basil leaves (ideally sunny, not refrigerator-cold), a peeled garlic clove, grated parmesan, good olive oil, a squeeze of lemon, pinch of salt. Pulse rather than blitz constantly — you want a rough paste, not a smooth sludge. Taste — salt, more lemon, more cheese until it sings.

steps.content: Cook pasta, reserve a splash of water.
steps.detail: Big pot, heavily salted water. Cook 1 min less than the packet says — the residual heat finishes it. Before you drain, scoop out half a cup of pasta water.

steps.content: Toss pasta with pesto off the heat.
steps.detail: KEY: off the heat. Hot pasta can cook the basil and dull the bright green. Drag pasta into the pesto with tongs, toss vigorously. Add splashes of reserved water as you go — the starch makes it loose and glossy rather than paste-clumped. More cheese on top, eat immediately while vivid.

title: Chicken Schnitty
description: Pub classic. Crunchy crumb, squeeze of lemon.

steps.content: Butterfly and bash chicken thin.
steps.detail: Lay breast flat, hold it down with one hand, slice horizontally through the middle to open it like a book — but don't cut all the way through. Open it out. Put between two sheets of baking paper and bash with a rolling pin or heavy pan till evenly 1cm thick. Even thickness = even cooking. Don't skip the paper; raw chicken shrapnel isn't the vibe.

steps.content: Flour, egg, crumb.
steps.detail: Three shallow plates: seasoned flour, 2 beaten eggs, breadcrumbs. Dredge each piece flour first (shake off excess — clumps go soggy), then egg (let drip), then press firmly into crumbs on both sides. Firm pressing = crumbs stick. Rest on a rack 5 min if you can — helps the crust set.

steps.content: Shallow-fry in butter, 4 min each side.
steps.detail: Wide pan, medium heat, enough butter + oil to come 5mm up the sides (butter adds flavour, oil stops it burning). When a crumb sizzles on contact, lay schnitties in — don't crowd. 4 min per side till the crumb is deep gold and the chicken is 72°C internal. Drain on paper towel.

steps.content: Serve with lemon wedges.
steps.detail: Plate hot, lemon wedges on the side. Squeeze over at the table — waiting lemon goes floppy on the crumb. Classic sides: chippies, a slaw, or a big pile of peas with butter. Anchovy mayo or tartare if you're fancy.

title: Sweet Potato Soup
description: Velvety, warming, crowd-pleasing.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic.
steps.detail: Finely dice a brown onion and grate 2 garlic cloves. Glug of olive oil in a heavy pot over medium. Onion with a pinch of salt, 6 min till translucent. Garlic for the last minute — any longer and it burns at the base of the pot. Bitter garlic = bitter soup.

steps.content: Add cubed sweet potato and cumin.
steps.detail: Peel sweet potato (or leave skin on — it's fine and nutritious) and cube into 3cm chunks. Even size = even cooking. Tip into the softened onion with a teaspoon of cumin. Stir 2 min to let the cumin wake up and coat the veg with oil.

steps.content: Pour in stock, simmer 20 min.
steps.detail: Pour in a litre of vegetable stock — should just cover the veg. Bring to a boil, drop to a simmer, lid slightly ajar. 20 min till sweet potato falls apart when prodded with a knife. If it's not completely tender, don't blitz yet — you'll end up with lumps.

steps.content: Blitz with coconut milk until silky.
steps.detail: Off heat. Add 200ml coconut milk. Stick blender straight in the pot — safest method, no transfers. Blitz 2 min till silky and velvety. If using a jug blender, work in batches and cover the lid with a tea towel (steam pressure pops lids). Taste — salt, pepper, a squeeze of lime lifts it. Reheat gently if needed.

title: Prawn Linguine
description: Garlic, chilli, white wine, parsley.

steps.content: Cook pasta.
steps.detail: Big pot of heavily salted water, rolling boil. Cook spaghetti 2 min less than packet — it'll finish in the sauce. Before draining, scoop a full cup of pasta water. That starchy liquid is the sauce's secret weapon for glossy clinginess.

steps.content: Sizzle garlic and chilli in oil.
steps.detail: Wide pan, good glug of olive oil, 4 garlic cloves thinly sliced, one fresh chilli sliced (or a pinch of flakes). Medium-LOW heat, not high. You want gentle sizzle for 2 min till garlic goes pale gold and fragrant — never dark. Dark garlic is bitter and ruins everything.

steps.content: Add prawns, cook 2 min, deglaze with wine.
steps.detail: Crank heat to medium-high. Pat prawns dry, tip in. They should sizzle. 60 sec one side till pink and curling, flip, 60 sec. Pour in half a cup of white wine — it'll bubble hard. Cook 90 sec till it reduces by half and loses its boozy sharpness.

steps.content: Toss with pasta, parsley, lemon.
steps.detail: Drain pasta straight into the pan with a big splash of reserved water. Torn parsley, juice of half a lemon, toss hard with tongs for 60 sec — water emulsifies with oil into a light sauce that cloaks every strand. Crack of pepper, eat immediately. No cheese with seafood pasta in Italy — prawns have their own sweetness.

title: Moroccan Lamb
description: Slow-spiced lamb with chickpeas, apricots, couscous.

steps.content: Brown lamb in batches.
steps.detail: Pat lamb cubes dry, season well with salt and pepper. Heat a glug of oil in a heavy pot till shimmering. Brown in BATCHES — crowding steams instead of sears. 3 min per side till each face is deep mahogany. Remove to a plate. That brown crust is the flavour foundation of the whole dish — don't rush it.

steps.content: Soften onions with spices.
steps.detail: Drop heat to medium. Tip in two finely diced brown onions with a pinch of salt. Scrape up any browned bits (fond) stuck to the pot — that's gravy gold. 8 min till soft. Stir in 2 tsp cumin and 1 tsp cinnamon for 30 sec to bloom the spices.

steps.content: Return lamb, add tomato, chickpeas, stock.
steps.detail: Lamb and any resting juices back in. Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand), a tin of drained chickpeas, and 500ml chicken stock. Stir, scraping the bottom. Should just cover the meat — add water if needed.

steps.content: Simmer 90 min, lid off last 20.
steps.detail: Bring to a simmer, lid on, drop heat to lowest. 70 min gentle simmer. Then lid off, turn heat up slightly, 20 min to reduce the sauce to a thick, glossy consistency that coats the lamb. Lamb should shred when poked with a fork. Taste for salt — slow cooks often need more.

steps.content: Scatter coriander.
steps.detail: Off heat. Tear a big handful of coriander over the top. Serve over couscous (cover dry couscous in equal hot water, rest 10 min, fluff) with a dollop of yoghurt. Harissa or chilli flakes for anyone who wants heat.

title: Breakfast Frittata
description: Sunday clean-out the fridge eggs.

steps.content: Par-boil diced potato.
steps.detail: Dice two potatoes into 1cm cubes. Into a pot of cold salted water (starting cold cooks them evenly). Bring to a boil, simmer 5 min — just till a knife goes through with slight resistance. They'll finish in the pan. Drain and pat dry; wet potato won't crisp.

steps.content: Fry onion and potato until golden.
steps.detail: Good glug of oil in an oven-safe pan (cast iron ideal) over medium-high. Tip in the par-boiled potatoes and a finely diced brown onion. Spread flat and leave 3 min for deep gold crust before tossing. Total 8 min till golden all over and onion is soft.

steps.content: Add spinach to wilt.
steps.detail: Two big handfuls of spinach straight into the hot pan. Toss with tongs — it'll wilt in 60 sec from two handfuls to a small pile. Surprising, but that's spinach for you.

steps.content: Pour over beaten eggs, dot with feta.
steps.detail: Beat 8 eggs with salt, pepper, splash of milk if you like a lighter set. Pour over the potato mix — don't stir; let the eggs pool and settle. Crumble feta on top in rough chunks. 3 min over medium till the edges are setting but the top is still wobbly.

steps.content: Finish under grill 5 min till puffed.
steps.detail: Straight under a hot grill for 5 min till puffed, set, and deep gold on top. Watch it — grills go fast. Rest 5 min in the pan before cutting into wedges. Eats hot, warm, or cold from the fridge tomorrow for lunch.

title: Steak & Chips
description: Hot pan, butter, a pile of chips. No frills.

steps.content: Roast chip-cut potatoes 25 min.
steps.detail: Cut potatoes into 1cm-thick chips, skin on. Toss on a tray with 3 tbsp oil, salt, pepper. Spread in a single layer — crowded chips steam. 220°C oven, 25 min, flipping once halfway. They're done when deeply gold with crispy edges and tender middles. Time this to finish just as the steaks rest.

steps.content: Pat steaks dry, season.
steps.detail: Take steaks out of the fridge 30 min before cooking — cold meat cooks unevenly. Pat BONE dry with paper towel, both sides. Surface moisture steams; we want searing. Season generously with sea salt flakes and cracked pepper, pressing it in. Don't oil the steak; oil the pan.

steps.content: Blazing hot pan, 3 min a side, butter basted.
steps.detail: Heavy pan (cast iron ideal) on the highest flame you've got till you see wisps of smoke. Splash of oil, steaks in. Don't touch for 3 min — let the crust form. Flip once. After flipping, add a knob of butter, smashed garlic, thyme. Tilt the pan and spoon the foaming butter over the top repeatedly for 2 min. For 2cm thick medium-rare.

steps.content: Rest 5 min. Serve with chips.
steps.detail: Lift steaks onto a board, tent loosely with foil. 5 min rest is NON-negotiable — juices redistribute into the meat instead of pooling on the board. Slice against the grain (perpendicular to muscle fibres) into thick slabs. Chips alongside, flaky salt, pan butter spooned over.

title: Veggie Wrap
description: Lunch for humans who hate the sad desk salad.

steps.content: Grill haloumi slices.
steps.detail: Slice haloumi into 1cm slabs. Pat dry — surface moisture prevents browning. Dry pan over medium-high, no oil. Lay slabs in and don't touch for 2 min for a gold crust. Flip once. Haloumi squeaks on the tooth while warm — eat fast.

steps.content: Warm wrap.
steps.detail: 10 sec on a dry pan per side, or 15 sec direct on a gas flame for a few char marks, or 10 sec in the microwave wrapped in a damp paper towel. Warm makes it pliable; cold wraps crack when you roll them.

steps.content: Layer with avo, haloumi, cucumber, rocket, feta.
steps.detail: Lay the wrap flat. Smash half an avocado down the middle third, leaving a 2cm gap at top and bottom. Haloumi on top, cucumber batons, a handful of rocket, crumbled feta. Don't overload — one layer each, or you won't be able to roll it.

steps.content: Roll tight, slice on the bias.
steps.detail: Fold the top and bottom edges in over the filling, then roll tightly from one side, tucking as you go. Press gently on the seam. Slice diagonally with a sharp knife — bias cut looks better and exposes the colourful cross-section. Eat with both hands.

title: Chilli Con Carne
description: Smoky, spicy, crowd-feeding.

steps.content: Brown mince with spices.
steps.detail: Hot heavy pot, splash of oil. Crumble 700g mince in in large chunks and don't touch for 3 min — let a deep brown crust form on the bottom. Then break up and cook till no pink remains, 5 min. Tip in 1 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp chilli powder. Stir 60 sec till spices bloom and the kitchen smells like a taqueria.

steps.content: Add diced onion and capsicum.
steps.detail: Push mince to the sides. In the clearing, add one finely diced brown onion and one finely diced red capsicum. Stir 6 min in the beef fat till soft. A big pinch of salt helps draw moisture out.

steps.content: Tip in tomatoes and beans.
steps.detail: Tip in two tins of tomatoes (crush whole ones by hand), two tins of beans (kidney and black bean is a great mix), drained and rinsed. Splash water from the tins in too. Stir, scraping the bottom for any stuck fond. Season, a square of dark chocolate or 1 tsp cocoa adds depth if you have it.

steps.content: Simmer 40 min.
steps.detail: Bring to a bubble, drop heat, lid half-on. 40 min gentle simmer, stirring every 10 min so it doesn't catch. It should thicken considerably — spoon dragged through leaves a trail. Taste for salt, a squeeze of lime at the end brightens everything.

steps.content: Serve with rice or corn chips.
steps.detail: Ladle over fluffy rice with a dollop of sour cream, grated cheese, sliced avocado, chopped coriander, lime wedge. Or build loaded corn chips — pile chilli over chips, blanket with cheese, grill till bubbly. Either way, the toppings matter.

title: Banana Bread
description: Speckled bananas, brown butter, the kindest loaf.

steps.content: Mash bananas.
steps.detail: Three very ripe bananas — brown-spotted or fully black is perfect; they're sweeter and softer. Peel and mash in a bowl with a fork till broken down but still a little chunky. Totally smooth loses the banana pockets in the finished loaf.

steps.content: Cream butter and sugar, add eggs.
steps.detail: In a stand mixer or with beaters: 100g soft butter and 3/4 cup sugar beaten 3 min till pale and fluffy — aeration is what gives the loaf a tender crumb. Add 2 eggs one at a time, beating between each. If it looks curdled, a tablespoon of the flour fixes it.

steps.content: Fold in bananas, flour, cinnamon.
steps.detail: Fold in the mashed banana with a spatula. Sift in 2 cups of self-raising flour and 1 tsp cinnamon. Fold in big sweeping strokes till JUST combined — overmixing develops gluten and gives you a tough, rubbery loaf. Streaks of flour are fine to stop at.

steps.content: Bake 55 min at 170°C.
steps.detail: Line a loaf tin with baking paper (overhang the sides so you can lift it out later). Pour in the batter, smooth the top. 170°C oven, middle shelf, 55 min. Done when a skewer in the centre comes out with a few moist crumbs but no raw batter. Cover with foil for the last 15 if the top is browning too fast.

steps.content: Cool in tin 15 min.
steps.detail: Rest in the tin 15 min — critical. Hot banana bread is fragile and will break if you tip it out too early. Then lift out with the paper onto a rack to cool fully before slicing. Butter on thick slices, or toasted the next day with more butter.

title: Poached Eggs
description: Vinegar, simmer, spoon, done. Eggs-ellence.

steps.content: Simmer water with a splash of vinegar.
steps.detail: Medium saucepan, 8cm of water, a dash of white vinegar (the acid helps the whites set fast). Bring to a bare simmer — tiny bubbles breaking the surface, NOT a rolling boil which will shred the eggs. A thermometer reads about 85°C.

steps.content: Swirl, slip in egg, 3 min.
steps.detail: Crack each egg into a small ramekin first — lets you see if a yolk breaks before committing. Stir the simmering water with a spoon to create a gentle whirlpool. Slide the egg into the centre of the swirl — the motion wraps the white around the yolk. 3 min for runny yolk and set whites. Don't poke.

steps.content: Lift, rest on tea towel.
steps.detail: Lift out with a slotted spoon, letting water drain. Rest briefly on a clean tea towel — catches the water that would otherwise make your toast soggy. Trim any raggedy white edges with scissors if you're plating fancy; nobody's watching at home though.

steps.content: Pile on buttered toast.
steps.detail: Thick-cut toast, buttered generously (butter goes on while toast is hot so it melts in). Egg perched on top, crack of sea salt, crack of pepper. Break the yolk with the edge of a knife and watch it flood over the butter. That's breakfast.

title: Greek Salad
description: Tomato, cucumber, olives, feta, oregano. Nothing hiding.

steps.content: Chunk tomato, cucumber, red onion.
steps.detail: Cut ripe tomatoes into irregular chunks (don't cube them — rustic is the point), cucumber into half-moons (halve lengthways, then slice), half a red onion thinly sliced (then soak in cold water 5 min if raw onion is too harsh for you, drain, pat dry).

steps.content: Toss with oil and lemon.
steps.detail: Big bowl. Toss the tomato, cucumber, onion with a generous glug of good olive oil and the juice of half a lemon. Pinch of salt, a scatter of dried oregano. The salt pulls juice from the tomato — that juice IS the dressing. Let it sit 5 min.

steps.content: Top with feta slab.
steps.detail: A proper Greek salad doesn't crumble the feta — it sits ON TOP as a whole slab. Lay a thick block of feta across the top of the bowl, drizzle with more oil, sprinkle of oregano. Presentation matters; it's a simple salad dressing up.

steps.content: Crack of pepper.
steps.detail: Generous crack of pepper over the feta and salad. A few kalamata olives if you have them. Eat with warm pita bread to mop up the juice at the bottom of the bowl — that's the best part. No cheese should ever touch a tomato without being present at a greek salad.

title: Hummus & Flatbread
description: Creamy chickpea dip. Snack-dinner energy.

steps.content: Blitz chickpeas, garlic, lemon, cumin.
steps.detail: Drain and rinse a tin of chickpeas — don't skip the rinse, the brine tastes tinny. For extra-creamy hummus, pinch the skins off each chickpea (tedious but transformative). Into a food processor with 2 garlic cloves, juice of a whole lemon, 1 tsp cumin, pinch of salt, 2 tbsp tahini if you have it.

steps.content: Stream in oil till silky.
steps.detail: Blitz on high for 60 sec to break everything down. Then WITH THE MOTOR RUNNING, stream in 1/3 cup of good olive oil slowly through the feed tube. This emulsifies the oil into the chickpea base for silky texture. 2 min total blending. Add a splash of iced water if it's stiff — cold water makes it whip up lighter.

steps.content: Warm wraps, tear, dip.
steps.detail: Warm wraps one at a time on a dry pan or direct gas flame for 10 sec a side till flexible with some char spots. Tear into tearable pieces and pile next to the hummus. Drizzle the hummus with more oil, a dust of paprika, a few chickpeas reserved for texture. Dip, inhale.

title: Carbonara
description: Eggs, parmesan, bacon, pepper. No cream. Fight me.

steps.content: Crisp bacon in a dry pan.
steps.detail: No oil needed — bacon renders its own fat. Lay strips in a cold pan, turn to medium. As the fat melts, the bacon shrinks and crisps. 5 min till deeply gold. Lift out with tongs. Leave the rendered fat in the pan — that's the sauce base.

steps.content: Cook spaghetti, reserve water.
steps.detail: Heavily salted water, rolling boil. Cook 1 min less than packet. Scoop out a full cup of pasta water before draining — carbonara needs more than most.

steps.content: Whisk eggs, parmesan, pepper.
steps.detail: In a bowl, whisk 2 whole eggs with finely grated parmesan (fine, microplane if you have it — smoother sauce) and a LOT of cracked black pepper. It should look like a thick paste. No cream, never. Set aside.

steps.content: OFF heat, toss pasta with egg mix.
steps.detail: This is the critical step. The pan must be off the heat and only warm — too hot and the eggs scramble. Drag drained pasta into the pan with tongs, toss to warm through 20 sec in the bacon fat. Then pour in the egg mix, toss vigorously. Splash in pasta water as you go — the starch emulsifies with the egg into a silky sauce that clings. Scrambled eggs = too hot. Silky = right.

steps.content: Fold in bacon, serve.
steps.detail: Tear bacon and fold through. More pepper on top, extra parmesan if you like. Eat the second it's plated — carbonara waits for no one.

title: Salmon Teriyaki
description: Sticky, glossy, rice underneath.

steps.content: Sear salmon skin-side down.
steps.detail: Pat salmon fillets dry, particularly the skin — wet skin NEVER crisps. Season both sides with salt. Hot pan, splash of oil, medium-high. Lay fillets in SKIN-SIDE DOWN and press gently with a spatula for 10 sec to stop them curling. Don't touch for 4 min — let the skin crisp to shatter-crunch. Flip for 90 sec on the flesh side. Remove to a plate.

steps.content: Mix soy, honey, grated ginger.
steps.detail: Small bowl: 3 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp honey, a thumb of freshly grated ginger. Whisk till the honey dissolves into the soy. Grating (not chopping) the ginger releases its juice — more flavour, no chewy fibres.

steps.content: Add sauce, reduce until sticky.
steps.detail: Same pan, drop heat to medium. Pour in the teriyaki mix — it'll hiss and bubble. Let it reduce 2 min till glossy and syrupy; it should coat the back of a spoon. Don't walk away; honey goes from glossy to burnt in seconds.

steps.content: Serve over rice with spring onion.
steps.detail: Salmon back in skin-side UP this time (preserves the crispy skin). Spoon the sticky sauce over the flesh a few times to glaze. 30 sec to warm through. Over a mound of jasmine rice, sliced spring onion scattered, sesame seeds if you have them. Steamed greens on the side.

title: Potato Salad
description: BBQ side dish of the gods.

steps.content: Boil potatoes till tender.
steps.detail: Cut potatoes into 3cm chunks (skin on — nutrition and texture). Into a pot of cold salted water — starting cold cooks them evenly. Bring to a boil, simmer 12 min till a knife slides through with no resistance. Drain and let the steam rise off for 2 min — dry potatoes soak up dressing better than wet ones.

steps.content: Boil eggs 8 min, cool, chop.
steps.detail: Lower eggs gently into a separate pot of simmering water. Timer for EXACTLY 8 min — just-set yolk, no grey ring. Straight into iced water to stop the cook and loosen the shell. Peel under a gentle stream, halve, then roughly chop.

steps.content: Toss warm potato with yoghurt, eggs, spring onion.
steps.detail: While potatoes are still WARM (they absorb dressing better warm than cold), tip into a big bowl with half a cup of yoghurt (or yoghurt + mayo half-half for richer), the chopped eggs, thinly sliced spring onions, 1 tsp dijon if you like a little kick. Fold gently — don't mash the potato or eggs.

steps.content: Season well, scatter parsley.
steps.detail: Big pinch of salt, crack of pepper, taste and adjust — potato is bland without enough salt. Tear parsley over the top. Rest 15 min before serving so the flavours meld; rest a full hour in the fridge and it's even better. A squeeze of lemon or splash of vinegar lifts the whole thing if it tastes flat.

title: Minestrone
description: Vegetables, beans, pasta. A ladle of summer in winter.

steps.content: Dice vegetables.
steps.detail: Small, even dice on everything — 1cm cubes on two carrots, two stalks of celery, one onion; grate a couple of garlic cloves. Small dice means a spoonful catches every vegetable and they cook at the same speed. This is the step that takes the longest; do it all before any heat goes on.

steps.content: Soften onion, carrot.
steps.detail: Glug of olive oil in a heavy pot over medium. Onion and carrot with a pinch of salt — soffritto. 8 min till onion is translucent and carrot is softening. This is the flavour foundation; don't rush. Add garlic the last minute.

steps.content: Add tomato, beans, stock, simmer 15 min.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush whole ones with your hand), a tin of drained beans, and a litre of vegetable stock. Chunks of zucchini too if you fancy. Bring to a simmer. 15 min uncovered for the veg to tenderise and the flavours to marry.

steps.content: Add pasta, cook 8 min.
steps.detail: A cup of small pasta (ditalini, broken spaghetti, whatever) straight into the simmering soup. 8 min till al dente. Pasta will keep soaking up broth as it sits, so serve within 20 min or make it a thick soup next day.

steps.content: Top with parmesan.
steps.detail: Ladle into bowls. Generous shower of grated parmesan — it melts into the soup and adds salty depth. Drizzle of good olive oil, crack of pepper, torn basil if you have it. Crusty bread alongside is mandatory.

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
description: Broth for the soul. Sick day cure.

steps.content: Poach chicken in stock with ginger, 20 min.
steps.detail: Chicken thighs (skin off, bone out — easier), a thumb of ginger sliced into coins, 1.5L chicken stock in a pot. Bring to a bare simmer — little bubbles, never a rolling boil or the chicken gets tough. 20 min and it's poached through. The broth becomes gingery and chickeny.

steps.content: Shred.
steps.detail: Lift chicken out with tongs onto a plate. When cool enough to handle, use two forks to pull it into strips along the grain. Or your hands if the meat's cool enough — rougher shreds soak up broth better than knife cuts.

steps.content: Cook noodles in broth.
steps.detail: Bring broth back to a simmer. Break spaghetti into thirds and drop straight in — cooking in stock not water means every strand soaks up flavour. Cook till just al dente per packet timing (usually 7 min).

steps.content: Return chicken, add carrot and spring onion.
steps.detail: Chicken back into the pot with thinly sliced carrot and sliced spring onions. 2 min to warm the chicken and barely soften the veg — you want a little bite in the carrot, not mush.

steps.content: Season well.
steps.detail: Taste and season well — soup always needs more salt than you think, and stock varies. Big pinch of salt, plenty of cracked pepper. A squeeze of lemon lifts it. Ladle into bowls piping hot; this is medicine.

title: Pork Larb
description: Thai mince salad. Herby, punchy, easy.

steps.content: Brown mince in dry pan till crisp.
steps.detail: Heat a dry pan (no oil) over medium-high. Crumble 500g pork mince in in big chunks. Cook without touching for 3 min for brown crust on the bottom, then break up with a wooden spoon and stir-fry another 4 min till it's deeply browned and some edges are crisp. Dry-frying concentrates the flavour — this is the core of larb.

steps.content: Off heat, toss with fish sauce, lime, herbs, chilli.
steps.detail: Off heat. Into a big bowl with the hot pork: 2 tbsp fish sauce, juice of two limes, three thinly sliced spring onions, a big handful each of mint and coriander (torn not chopped — bruising turns herbs black), one sliced fresh chilli. Toss. Taste — should hit salty, sour, herby, hot. Adjust in tiny increments.

steps.content: Spoon into lettuce cups.
steps.detail: Peel off whole leaves of iceberg lettuce for cups — cup them in your palm, spoon in a heaped tablespoon of warm larb, top with more herbs if you like. Two-bite snacks. Eat with your hands; sticky rice or jasmine rice on the side is traditional.

title: Eggplant Parma
description: The vego pub hero. Crumbed, tomato, bubbly cheese.

steps.content: Slice zucchini lengthways.
steps.detail: Slice a zucchini lengthways into 1cm-thick planks. Salt both sides lightly and rest on paper towel 10 min — pulls out bitter moisture. Pat dry. Even thickness matters; wobbly slices cook unevenly.

steps.content: Flour, egg, crumb, fry golden.
steps.detail: Three shallow plates: seasoned flour, 2 beaten eggs, breadcrumbs. Dredge each plank flour (shake off), egg (let drip), crumbs (press firmly both sides). Shallow fry in 5mm of hot oil over medium-high, 2 min a side till deep gold. Drain on paper.

steps.content: Layer in dish with tomato and mozzarella.
steps.detail: Baking dish — spread a ladle of napoli (gently simmered crushed tomato with garlic, salt, pepper) across the base. Lay crumbed zucchini planks on top in one layer. Spoon more sauce over each, then tear mozzarella into chunks and dot over.

steps.content: Bake 25 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: 200°C oven, middle rack, 25 min till the cheese is deeply bubbling and blistered with gold patches. If it's pale, flash under the grill for 2 min — watch it; cheese goes from gold to burnt fast.

steps.content: Basil to finish.
steps.detail: Torn basil leaves scattered just before serving — adding them before the oven turns them black. Crack of pepper, a final drizzle of good olive oil. Crusty bread and a green salad on the side.

title: Breakfast Burrito
description: Eggs, beans, cheese, all rolled up.

steps.content: Scramble eggs.
steps.detail: Crack 4 eggs into a bowl and beat with salt, pepper, a splash of milk for lightness. Warm a knob of butter in a non-stick pan over low heat — low is the secret to silky scramble. Pour eggs in. As they start to set at the edges, fold gently with a spatula in big slow strokes. Off heat while still glossy and slightly underdone — residual heat finishes them.

steps.content: Warm beans, mash lightly.
steps.detail: Drain a tin of beans (black or pinto), tip into a small pan with a splash of water, pinch of cumin, pinch of salt. Warm 3 min. Mash about half with a fork for texture — some whole, some mashed binds the filling so it doesn't spill from the tortilla.

steps.content: Pile onto warm tortillas with cheese, avo.
steps.detail: Warm tortillas one at a time on a dry pan for 15 sec a side till pliable. Down the middle third: eggs, beans, grated cheese, slices of avocado, torn coriander, hot sauce. Don't overload — overstuffed burritos explode.

steps.content: Roll tight, sear seam-side down.
steps.detail: Fold the top and bottom edges in over the filling, then roll tightly from one side — tuck the filling in with your fingers as you go. Place seam-side-down in a hot dry pan for 60 sec — seals the roll and toasts the outside. Flip once. Slice diagonally.

title: Stir-Fry Beef
description: Fridge-emptying weeknight. Hot wok, fast hands.

steps.content: Slice beef thin, marinate in soy and ginger.
steps.detail: Freeze the beef 15 min first — much easier to slice thin. Cut ACROSS the grain (perpendicular to muscle fibres) into 3mm strips. In a bowl, toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tsp cornflour if you have it. Rest 10 min while you prep everything else. Cornflour velvetises the meat.

steps.content: Blast in hot wok, 1 min, remove.
steps.detail: Wok smoking-hot — a drop of water should vaporise on contact. 1 tbsp oil, swirl to coat. Tip the beef in in a single layer. Don't stir for 60 sec — let it sear. Then toss fast for 60 sec more till edges are deep brown but centres still pink. Out to a plate. Crowding = steam; you might need to work in batches.

steps.content: Stir-fry veg, add garlic.
steps.detail: Same wok, more oil. Drop heat slightly. Broccoli florets, sliced capsicum go in first — they take longer. 2 min tossing. Then 3 grated garlic cloves and a thumb of grated ginger. 30 sec. Veg should be bright and slightly charred, still with crunch.

steps.content: Return beef, splash in oyster sauce.
steps.detail: Beef back in with any resting juices. Splash in 3 tbsp oyster sauce. Toss 60 sec till everything's glossy and coated. Off heat, drizzle sesame oil.

steps.content: Over rice.
steps.detail: Pile over hot jasmine rice. Sliced spring onion scattered on top. Chilli flakes or fresh chilli if you want heat. Eat immediately while the wok heat is still in the dish.

title: Greek Lamb Souva
description: Skewers, tzatziki, warm bread. Backyard Friday.

steps.content: Cube lamb, skewer, marinate in oil and lemon.
steps.detail: Cut lamb into 3cm cubes — same-size pieces cook at the same speed. Thread onto skewers (soak wooden ones in water 30 min first or they'll char to dust). Leave a little gap between cubes so the heat gets in. In a bowl, toss with olive oil, the juice and zest of a lemon, 2 grated garlic cloves, dried oregano, salt, pepper. 30 min minimum; overnight is transformative.

steps.content: Grate cucumber into yoghurt with garlic and mint.
steps.detail: Grate half a cucumber on the coarse side, then squeeze the shreds hard in a tea towel to get rid of the water — watery tzatziki is sad tzatziki. Tip squeezed cucumber into a bowl with 1 cup yoghurt, 2 grated garlic cloves, big handful chopped mint, juice of half a lemon, pinch salt. Stir. Rest 10 min for flavours to meld.

steps.content: Grill skewers 8 min.
steps.detail: BBQ or griddle pan blazing hot. Oil the grills. Skewers on, don't move for 2 min — let char marks form. Turn every 2 min, 8 min total for pink in the middle on 3cm cubes (lamb is best medium-rare). Rest 3 min on a board — juices redistribute, meat stays juicy.

steps.content: Serve in warm flatbread with tzatziki.
steps.detail: Warm flatbreads 15 sec a side on the BBQ. Smear tzatziki down the middle, slide lamb off the skewers on top. Sliced tomato, red onion, extra oregano, squeeze of lemon. Roll tight, eat standing up.

title: Fried Egg Sandwich
description: Yolk, butter, sauce of your choosing.

steps.content: Butter bread, scrape one side with vegemite.
steps.detail: Two slices of soft white bread (sourdough works too if you want grown-up). Butter generously on ONE side — butter side goes inside. On the other slice, scrape the thinnest layer of vegemite — it's powerful, a smear not a blanket. Balance is everything.

steps.content: Fry eggs to your liking.
steps.detail: Hot non-stick pan, knob of butter. Crack 2 eggs straight in. For runny yolks: cover with a lid for 90 sec — steam cooks the tops while bottoms crisp. For over-easy: flip once carefully, 10 sec. For well-done: 3 min uncovered. Your call.

steps.content: Sandwich. Mess is expected.
steps.detail: Eggs onto the vegemite slice, butter slice on top (buttered side DOWN, touching egg). Press gently. Yolk breaks and floods the bread — that's the whole point. Eat over a plate. Mess is expected.

title: Mac & Cheese
description: Béchamel, cheddar, pasta, bubbly top. Comfort.

steps.content: Cook pasta.
steps.detail: Big pot of heavily salted water, rolling boil. Cook macaroni 2 min LESS than packet — it'll finish in the oven and you don't want mush. Drain. Don't rinse — the starch helps sauce cling.

steps.content: Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, milk.
steps.detail: Same pot, medium heat. Melt 60g butter. Whisk in 60g flour (equal weights = classic roux). Cook 2 min stirring constantly — the paste turns blonde and smells nutty. Raw flour tastes chalky; cook it out. Slowly pour in 750ml warmed milk in a steady stream, whisking constantly. It'll thicken as it heats. Bring to a bare simmer; cook 5 min till it coats the back of a spoon.

steps.content: Off heat, melt in cheese.
steps.detail: OFF THE HEAT. Handful at a time, whisk in 250g grated cheddar till melted and smooth. If the pan's too hot when cheese goes in, the sauce splits and goes grainy. A grating of nutmeg, a teaspoon of dijon, salt, pepper.

steps.content: Fold in pasta.
steps.detail: Tip drained pasta into the cheese sauce, fold through till every macaroni is coated in cheese silk. Looks loose? Good — it firms up in the oven.

steps.content: Top with crumbs, bake 15 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: Transfer to a baking dish. Scatter breadcrumbs (buttered if you want extra crunch — toss crumbs with 1 tbsp melted butter first) across the top, a little more cheese. 200°C, 15 min till the top is deeply gold and the edges are bubbling. Rest 5 min — molten cheese burns tongues.

title: Lentil Bolognese
description: All the depth of a slow ragù. Zero meat.

steps.content: Soften diced carrot, onion, garlic.
steps.detail: Finely dice 2 carrots, 1 onion, 3 garlic cloves. Small dice = every spoonful catches all three. Glug of olive oil in a heavy pot over medium. Veg in with a pinch of salt, 10 min till onion is translucent and carrot is softening. This soffritto is the depth; don't rush.

steps.content: Add lentils, stock, tomatoes.
steps.detail: Tip in 1.5 cups of red lentils (rinsed till the water runs clearer), 500ml vegetable stock, 2 tins of tomatoes (crush with your hand). Splash water into the empty tins to rinse out the rest and tip that in too. Big pinch of salt, pepper, bay leaf if you have one.

steps.content: Simmer 35 min till thick.
steps.detail: Bring to a bubble, drop to a simmer, lid slightly ajar. 35 min stirring every 10 min so lentils don't catch on the bottom. They break down into a thick, meaty-textured ragu. A splash of milk or a knob of butter at the end rounds the edges off the tomato acid.

steps.content: Toss with cooked spaghetti.
steps.detail: Cook spaghetti 1 min less than the packet in heavily salted water. Reserve half a cup of pasta water. Toss pasta into the sauce with a splash of water — creates the emulsion that makes the sauce cling. Plate up, generous parmesan (use nutritional yeast for vegan), crack of pepper. Torn basil.

title: Roast Veg Couscous
description: Meal-prep friendly, pretty, bright.

steps.content: Chop and roast veg 30 min, 200°C.
steps.detail: Cube sweet potato into 2cm chunks, red onion into wedges, zucchini into half-moons, capsicum into rough 3cm pieces. Spread on a tray in a single layer — crowded veg steam instead of caramelise. Toss with a good glug of oil, salt, pepper, any dried herbs you have. 200°C, 30 min, shaking the tray halfway.

steps.content: Make couscous per packet.
steps.detail: Per packet usually: equal parts couscous and boiling water (or stock for more flavour), salt, splash of oil. Cover with a plate or foil, rest 10 min. Fluff vigorously with a fork to separate grains. Cous-cous loves a fluff — pressed-together grains become a lump.

steps.content: Toss veg through couscous with lemon.
steps.detail: Tip the hot roasted veg straight onto the couscous while everything is still warm — warm absorbs dressing better. Juice and zest of a lemon, extra glug of olive oil, toss with a big spoon. Taste — more salt if flat, more lemon if rich.

steps.content: Crumble feta on top.
steps.detail: Crumble feta over the top in rough chunks — don't stir it through or it disappears. Pine nuts or almonds toasted dry 3 min in a pan add crunch. Mint or parsley torn over. Eats warm or fridge-cold tomorrow for lunchboxes.

title: Chicken Parma
description: Schnitty, napoli, ham, cheese. The classic.

steps.content: Crumb and shallow-fry schnitties.
steps.detail: Butterfly breasts (slice horizontally to open like a book, don't cut all the way), bash between baking paper till 1cm thick. Flour-egg-crumb each piece, pressing firmly. Shallow-fry in 5mm of butter-oil mix over medium, 3 min a side till deep gold. Drain on paper. You want cooked-through but crumb only just set — it finishes in the oven.

steps.content: Reduce tomato with garlic, salt.
steps.detail: Same pan, drop heat to medium. Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand) and 2 grated garlic cloves. Big pinch of salt, teaspoon of sugar if it's sharp. Simmer 10 min till thickened to a spoonable sauce. Taste — should be punchy; it stands up to cheese.

steps.content: Top each schnitty with napoli, ham, mozzarella.
steps.detail: Schnitties on a baking tray. Ladle napoli over each (generous but not drowning — edges should still peek). Lay a slice of ham on each. Then tear mozzarella into chunks and dot over, letting some hang over the edges where it'll crisp. Optional: handful of grated parmesan for extra sharp.

steps.content: Grill till melted.
steps.detail: Under a hot grill (top heat) for 4–6 min till the cheese is bubbling and deeply blistered with gold spots — some char is the whole point of parma. Watch it — cheese goes from gold to burnt in seconds. Rest 2 min. Chippies and salad alongside is pub-law.

title: Overnight Oats
description: Tomorrow-you will thank today-you.

steps.content: Mix oats, milk, yoghurt, honey in a jar.
steps.detail: Into a jar or a container with a tight lid: 1/2 cup rolled oats (not instant — instant goes gluey), 1/2 cup milk (dairy or oat or whatever you drink), 1/3 cup yoghurt, 1 tsp honey. Stir thoroughly so no dry pockets remain. The oats will swell and thicken overnight; if it looks too liquidy now, that's normal.

steps.content: Top with berries.
steps.detail: Scatter 1/2 cup berries over the top — frozen is fine, they'll half-thaw by morning and bleed colour into the oats. Don't stir them through yet; keep them visible on top for when you open the jar tomorrow. A sprinkle of chia seeds adds a pudding-like texture if you have some.

steps.content: Lid on, fridge overnight.
steps.detail: Lid on tight. Fridge at least 4 hours, ideally overnight. Morning-you opens it, gives it a stir (oats will be thick and creamy), maybe adds more honey or a splash of milk if it's too thick. Extra toppings in the morning: sliced banana, chopped nuts, a spoon of peanut butter. Eats cold or warmed 30 sec in the microwave if you're a warm-breakfast person.

title: Pancakes
description: Stack, butter, syrup. Weekend mandate.

steps.content: Whisk dry, then wet ingredients.
steps.detail: In one bowl: 1.5 cups self-raising flour, 2 tbsp sugar, pinch of salt. Whisk to break up lumps. In a separate jug: 1.5 cups milk, 1 egg, 50g melted cooled butter. Beat with a fork. Tip wet into dry and whisk just till combined — lumps are FINE. Overmixed batter makes tough, flat pancakes. Gluten is the enemy here.

steps.content: Rest 10 min.
steps.detail: Cover the bowl with a tea towel and leave on the bench 10 min. The flour hydrates, the baking powder activates, the gluten relaxes. Skipping this gives you chewy pancakes. While it rests, get a warm plate ready in the oven (lowest setting) for stacking finished ones.

steps.content: Cook 2 min a side in buttered pan.
steps.detail: Non-stick pan over medium — not high, high burns outside before inside cooks. Swirl a knob of butter. Ladle in 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Wait till bubbles form and pop across the surface, 2 min. Flip once — second side cooks faster, about 90 sec. Don't press down with the spatula; squashes the air out.

steps.content: Stack unreasonably high.
steps.detail: Stack on that warm plate — five or six high, impressive is the whole point. Pat of butter on top of the stack so it melts down between the layers. Maple syrup poured generously. Berries or sliced banana if you're feeling wholesome. Eat fast; nobody likes cold pancakes.

title: Fish & Chips
description: Beer batter, crispy chips, fresh lemon.

steps.content: Cut chips, twice-fry or oven-bake.
steps.detail: Cut potatoes into 1cm-thick chips, skin on. For twice-fry: first fry in 160°C oil for 6 min till soft but pale, drain, then blast at 190°C for 3 min till deeply gold and crisp. For oven: toss with 3 tbsp oil, salt, spread on a tray, 220°C for 30 min flipping once. Start the chips first — they take longest.

steps.content: Make batter with flour, beer, salt.
steps.detail: In a bowl, whisk 1 cup plain flour, 1 tsp baking powder, pinch of salt with 1 cup of cold beer. Cold is key — the cold batter hits hot oil for maximum crisp. Whisk just till smooth; a few lumps are fine. Should be the consistency of pouring cream. Rest 5 min.

steps.content: Dip fish, fry 4 min till crisp.
steps.detail: Heat 5cm of neutral oil in a heavy pot to 180°C (a cube of bread should turn gold in 30 sec). Pat fish fillets dry, dust lightly in flour first (helps batter cling). Dip in batter, let excess drip, lower gently into oil away from you. 4 min till deep gold and crisp. Lift onto paper towel.

steps.content: Serve with lemon.
steps.detail: Lemon wedges alongside — squeezed over JUST before eating. Malt vinegar and tartare sauce if you've got them. Eat on newspaper (or a plate if you're civilised) with the chips piled next to. Salt hot chips immediately — hot surface absorbs salt better than cool. The whole deal eaten within 10 min.

title: Thai Beef Salad
description: Rare beef, herbs, chilli, lime. Summer dinner.

steps.content: Sear steak rare, rest, slice.
steps.detail: Pat steak dry, season heavily with salt and pepper. Hot pan, splash of oil. Sear 90 sec a side — rare is traditional, the warm beef cooks slightly in the dressing. 2 min a side for medium-rare if rare isn't your thing. Rest 5 min on a board (this is critical — slicing straight away loses all the juice). Slice very thin ACROSS the grain.

steps.content: Toss veg and herbs with fish sauce, lime, chilli.
steps.detail: In the biggest bowl you have: half a cucumber cut into chunks, 200g cherry tomatoes halved, big handful each of mint and coriander torn (not chopped), sliced red onion. Pour over the dressing: 2 tbsp fish sauce, juice of 2 limes, 1 tsp sugar, 1 thinly sliced fresh chilli. Toss gently. Taste — salty, sour, a touch sweet, hot. Balance is everything.

steps.content: Fan beef over the top.
steps.detail: Fan beef slices over the top of the dressed salad on a big platter — DON'T toss through. The beef wants to sit on top for presentation and so the juices mingle with the dressing. Pour any resting juices from the board over the top. Extra herbs, extra chilli if you love heat, crushed peanuts for crunch.

title: Butter Chicken
description: Melting tandoori-spiced chicken in a silky butter-tomato sauce.

steps.content: Marinate chicken in yoghurt, turmeric, curry powder, 30 min.
steps.detail: Cube chicken thigh into 3cm pieces. In a bowl: yoghurt, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp curry powder, salt, a squeeze of lemon if you have it. Mix with the chicken till every piece is coated. 30 min fridge is good; overnight is transformative — yoghurt's lactic acid tenderises the meat while the spices penetrate.

steps.content: Sear chicken in butter, set aside.
steps.detail: Knob of butter in a heavy pan over medium-high till foaming.
