Shake excess marinade off each piece of chicken and sear in batches — overcrowding steams instead of sears. 2 min a side for deep gold char patches. Chicken isn't fully cooked yet — it finishes in the sauce. Remove to a plate.

steps.content: Soften onion, garlic, ginger.
steps.detail: Same pan, don't wash. Drop heat to medium. More butter if needed. One finely diced onion, 3 grated garlic cloves, a thumb of grated ginger. 6 min till soft and golden at edges. The fond from the chicken lifts into this base.

steps.content: Blend tomato smooth, add with cream, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Tip a tin of tomatoes into a blender and blitz smooth (or push through a sieve for silky texture). Pour into the pan with the onion mix. 1/2 cup cream stirred through. Bring to a bare simmer — don't boil hard once cream's in or it can split. 10 min till thickened and the raw tomato edge is gone.

steps.content: Return chicken, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Chicken back in with any resting juices. Simmer very gently for 10 min till the chicken is cooked through (check by cutting one in half — no pink). Taste for salt and a pinch of sugar if the tomato is sharp. A knob of extra butter at the end rounds it to silk.

steps.content: Serve over basmati with coriander.
steps.detail: Fluff basmati. Ladle butter chicken into shallow bowls, rice alongside (not under — it goes mushy). Tear a big handful of coriander over the top. Naan if you have it, for mopping the sauce. A dollop of extra yoghurt cools the spice for anyone who wants it.

title: Lamb Kofta with Tzatziki
description: Spiced lamb skewers, cool yoghurt, warm flatbread. Friday night on the grill.

steps.content: Grate onion, squeeze dry.
steps.detail: Grate a red onion on the coarse side of a box grater into a sieve or tea towel. Squeeze HARD to remove the juice — wet onion makes kofta fall apart on the grill. Liquid out, oniony pulp left behind. Counterintuitive but essential.

steps.content: Mix lamb, onion, garlic, spices, half the mint.
steps.detail: Big bowl: 700g lamb mince, the squeezed grated onion, 3 grated garlic cloves, 2 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, salt, pepper, half a handful of finely chopped mint. Mix with your hands for 60 sec — till it tightens up and becomes sticky. That stickiness is the protein binding, which keeps kofta on the skewer.

steps.content: Shape onto skewers, chill 10 min.
steps.detail: Divide into 8 portions. Shape each around a skewer (soak wooden skewers 30 min first, or use metal) into a long 2cm-thick sausage. Press firmly so it clings. Chill 10 min — firms them up so they don't slide off on the grill. Skipping this step means half your kofta in the coals.

steps.content: Grate cucumber into yoghurt with remaining mint and garlic for tzatziki.
steps.detail: Grate half a cucumber on the coarse side, squeeze HARD to remove water. Tip into a bowl with 1 cup yoghurt, 2 grated garlic cloves, big handful chopped mint, juice of half a lemon, pinch of salt. Stir. This is tzatziki — rest 10 min so flavours meld.

steps.content: Grill skewers 4 min a side.
steps.detail: BBQ or griddle pan blazing hot, oil the grills. Kofta on, don't move for 2 min — let char marks form. Turn once. 4 min per side for pink in the middle, 5 for medium. They should feel firm but still give a little. Rest 3 min on a board.

steps.content: Wrap with tzatziki and lemon.
steps.detail: Warm flatbreads 15 sec a side on the hot grill. Smear tzatziki down the middle, slide two kofta off the skewer on top. Squeeze of lemon, sliced tomato, extra mint. Roll tight, eat standing up. Mess is expected.

title: Pork Banh Mi
description: Crackly roll, spicy pork, pickled veg, coriander. Vietnamese sandwich royalty.

steps.content: Slice pork thin, marinate in soy, sriracha, lime.
steps.detail: Freeze pork belly 15 min for easier slicing. Cut into 5mm slices across the grain. In a bowl, toss with 2 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp sriracha, juice of half a lime, a teaspoon of sugar. 15 min marinade while you prep everything else. Don't skip the sugar — it helps caramelisation.

steps.content: Pickle julienned carrot and cucumber in vinegar and sugar, 15 min.
steps.detail: Julienne one carrot and half a cucumber (thin matchsticks, 5cm long). Into a bowl with 1/3 cup vinegar, 2 tbsp sugar, pinch salt, splash of water. Submerge the veg. 15 min minimum — the crunch stays but the sharpness mellows and the acid cuts through rich pork.

steps.content: Sear pork in hot pan till caramelised.
steps.detail: Hot pan, splash of oil. Pork in a single layer, don't crowd — it'll stew instead of sear if piled up. 90 sec one side till deep caramelised, flip, 60 sec the other. Sugar in the marinade will hit the pan and catch easily — medium-high not max.

steps.content: Split baguettes, spread mayo, stack pork, pickles, coriander, chilli.
steps.detail: Split a crusty baguette lengthways — don't cut all the way through, hinge it open. Smear mayo on one cut side, sriracha on the other if you want extra heat. Stack pork, then drained pickles, a fistful of coriander, sliced fresh chilli. Close tightly. Crackly bread, cool pickles, rich pork, herby fresh — that's banh mi.

title: Rainbow Quinoa Salad
description: Fluffy quinoa, roast pumpkin, beetroot, feta, seeds. Meal-prep gold.

steps.content: Roast diced pumpkin and beetroot 30 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: Cube pumpkin into 2cm chunks, beetroot into 1.5cm (smaller — beets take longer). Line a tray so beet juice doesn't stain. Toss with 2 tbsp oil, salt, pepper. Spread in a single layer. 200°C oven, 30 min till edges are caramelised and a knife slides through easily. The pumpkin will be golden, beets deep purple.

steps.content: Rinse and cook quinoa in 3 cups water, 15 min.
steps.detail: Rinse 1.5 cups quinoa in a fine sieve under cold water for 30 sec — washes off a natural bitter coating called saponin. Skip it and the salad tastes soapy. Into a pot with 3 cups water, pinch salt, bring to a boil, lid on, drop to low. 15 min till you see little curled tails on each grain. Off heat 5 min covered, then fluff.

steps.content: Whisk lemon juice with olive oil for dressing.
steps.detail: In a jar: juice and zest of a lemon, 3 tbsp olive oil, pinch of salt, pinch of sugar if you want to round off the lemon. Shake till emulsified (creamy-thick, not separated). Taste — should be bright. Too sharp? More oil or a drizzle of honey.

steps.content: Toss quinoa, roast veg, rocket, mint with dressing.
steps.detail: Biggest bowl you have. Cooked quinoa (room temp is fine), roasted veg (slightly warm is great — absorbs dressing better), three handfuls of rocket, torn mint. Pour over the dressing and toss gently with tongs — don't mash the beets into everything or it all turns pink.

steps.content: Crumble feta, scatter almonds.
steps.detail: Crumble feta generously over the top — don't stir it through or it disappears. Scatter toasted almonds for crunch (toast almonds dry in a pan 3 min till fragrant). Eats warm, room temp, or fridge-cold tomorrow for lunch. Better the next day as the flavours meld.

title: Sticky Date Pudding
description: The pub classic. Dark, toffee-soaked sponge. Serve with cream, obviously.

steps.content: Soak dates in 1 cup boiling water, 10 min.
steps.detail: Pit dates if they're not already (you'll know — if you bite one, you'll know fast). Chop roughly. Cover with 1 cup boiling water and 1 tsp bicarb (the bicarb breaks down the dates and neutralises their acidity). Rest 10 min — they swell and soften into a paste. Mash gently with a fork.

steps.content: Cream butter and sugar, beat in eggs.
steps.detail: In a stand mixer or with electric beaters: 125g soft butter and 1 cup brown sugar, beat 3 min till pale and fluffy. Pale = aerated = tender crumb. Add 2 eggs one at a time, beating between. If it looks curdled, a tablespoon of flour fixes it.

steps.content: Fold in flour, baking powder, date mix, vanilla.
steps.detail: Sift in 1.5 cups self-raising flour and 1 tsp baking powder. Fold with a spatula till just combined — don't beat. Add the date mix (with liquid) and 1 tsp vanilla. Fold gently. Batter should be thick and pourable.

steps.content: Bake 30 min at 180°C.
steps.detail: Line a square 20cm tin with baking paper (overhang for easy lifting). Pour in batter, smooth top. 180°C oven, middle rack, 30 min. Skewer comes out with a few moist crumbs, not raw batter. It'll sink slightly in the middle — that's perfect for catching sauce.

steps.content: Simmer brown sugar, butter, cream for sauce.
steps.detail: Same pot, no wash needed. Melt 120g butter with 1 cup brown sugar and 300ml thickened cream. Whisk till the sugar dissolves. Bring to a bare simmer for 3 min till glossy and thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Rich, toffee-like, dangerous.

steps.content: Pour over warm pudding, serve with thickened cream.
steps.detail: Poke holes in the warm pudding with a skewer. Pour half the hot sauce over — it soaks in and disappears. Let it drink. Slice into squares, plate warm with the rest of the sauce poured on top and a dollop of thickened cream or ice cream. Don't refrigerate the sauce — it sets like toffee.

title: Pumpkin & Sage Risotto
description: Sweet roast pumpkin folded through creamy rice, crispy sage on top.

steps.content: Roast pumpkin 25 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: Cube 600g pumpkin into 2cm chunks, toss with olive oil, salt, pepper. Spread on a tray in a single layer — crowded pumpkin steams instead of caramelises. 200°C oven, 25 min till edges are deeply gold and a knife slides through easily. Start this first; runs in parallel with the risotto base.

steps.content: Fry sage in butter till crisp, remove.
steps.detail: Handful of sage leaves (whole, not torn). Knob of butter in a small pan over medium till foaming. Sage in, 30 sec a side till vivid green and translucent-crisp — they shatter when you bite. Lift onto paper towel with tongs, sprinkle with flaky salt. Reserve the sage butter in the pan for later.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic.
steps.detail: Finely dice a brown onion, grate 2 garlic cloves. Heavy wide pan over medium, splash of oil. Onion with a pinch of salt, 8 min till soft and translucent — don't brown. Garlic for the last minute. Go gentle; this is the sweet base.

steps.content: Toast rice, deglaze with wine.
steps.detail: Tip in 1.5 cups of short-grain rice. Stir 2 min till every grain is glossy and edges turn slightly translucent — that's toasting. Pour in 1/2 cup white wine, stir till absorbed and the raw alcohol has cooked off, 2 min.

steps.content: Ladle in stock gradually, stirring, 20 min.
steps.detail: Warm stock in a separate pot — cold stock shocks the rice. Ladle into the risotto pan, just enough to cover the rice, stir till absorbed, repeat. 20 min total. Stirring releases starch — that's the creaminess. Rice should be tender with a slight bite at the centre.

steps.content: Fold through roast pumpkin and parmesan.
steps.detail: Off heat. Fold in the roasted pumpkin (some will break down into the rice, some stays in chunks — both good). 50g grated parmesan, a knob of butter. Stir vigorously — that's mantecatura, which brings the silk. Cover, rest 2 min.

steps.content: Top with crispy sage.
steps.detail: Plate in shallow bowls — risotto should spread slowly like lava, not stand up stiff. If it's too thick, a splash of warm stock loosens. Sage leaves perched on top, drizzle of the sage butter from earlier, extra parmesan shavings. Cracked pepper.

title: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Kra Pao)
description: Fiery minced chicken with holy basil, over rice, crowned with a crispy egg.

steps.content: Mince garlic and chilli together.
steps.detail: Into a mortar and pestle (or food processor pulsed): 4 garlic cloves and 3 fresh chillies. Pound together into a rough paste. This fusion of garlic and chilli is the base of all Thai stir-fries — pounding releases oils that chopping doesn't. If using a knife, chop them together finely so the flavours marry.

steps.content: Chop chicken roughly.
steps.detail: Instead of mince, Thai kitchens chop chicken thigh by hand into 5mm dice — gives better texture than a processor. Use a heavy knife on a sturdy board. If short on time, pulse briefly in a food processor. Never go to paste.

steps.content: Fry garlic-chilli, add chicken, cook till browned.
steps.detail: Wok smoking-hot, 1 tbsp oil, swirl. Garlic-chilli paste in for 30 sec till fragrant — don't let it brown. Chicken in, break up with the spatula, and stir-fry hard for 3 min till no pink remains and some edges are caramelised. High heat and fast hands.

steps.content: Add sauces and sugar, toss in basil at the last second.
steps.detail: Splash in 2 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tsp sugar. Toss 30 sec till the sauce coats everything and reduces to glossy. Off heat (so the basil doesn't go black), tip in 2 handfuls of Thai basil leaves (regular basil is fine), toss once — they'll wilt from the residual heat.

steps.content: Fry an egg till edges crisp.
steps.detail: Non-stick pan with 2 tbsp oil over medium-high. Crack an egg in, don't touch for 2 min — you want crispy, lacy edges while the yolk stays runny. Thai fried eggs are all about the frilly edges. Salt and pepper.

steps.content: Serve over rice, egg on top.
steps.detail: Hot jasmine rice in a bowl, chicken mix piled alongside (not over — you want to see both), crispy egg balanced on top. Break the yolk with your fork, it floods the rice. Extra fish sauce with sliced chilli (nam pla prik) on the side for those who want more heat.

title: Fudgy Chocolate Brownies
description: Crinkle top, molten middle. The kind you eat straight from the tin.

steps.content: Melt chocolate and butter gently.
steps.detail: Bowl over barely-simmering water (don't let the water touch the bowl). 200g chopped dark chocolate and 200g butter. Stir occasionally till smooth and glossy — about 4 min. Off heat, let cool 5 min. Hot chocolate hitting cold eggs will scramble them.

steps.content: Whisk in sugar, then eggs and vanilla.
steps.detail: Whisk 1.5 cups sugar into the cooled chocolate till dissolved. Crack in 3 eggs one at a time, whisking well between each — you want the whole mix glossy and slightly airy. The more you whisk the eggs now, the shinier the crinkle top. 1 tsp vanilla in.

steps.content: Fold in flour, cocoa, chopped walnuts.
steps.detail: Sift 1 cup flour and 1/4 cup cocoa directly over the chocolate mix. Pinch of salt. Fold with a spatula till JUST combined — big gentle strokes, don't stir or whisk. Overmixing develops gluten and makes brownies cakey instead of fudgy. Fold in chopped walnuts on the last stroke.

steps.content: Pour into lined tin.
steps.detail: Line a 20cm square tin with baking paper, overhanging edges for easy lifting. Pour batter in, smooth the top with a spatula. Give the tin a gentle bang on the bench — knocks air pockets out.

steps.content: Bake 22 min at 180°C — middle should still wobble.
steps.detail: 180°C oven, middle rack, 22 min. The middle should still wobble when you shake the tin — it looks underdone but it sets as it cools. Overbaked brownies are cakey and sad. A skewer should come out with thick batter on it. That's what you want.

steps.content: Cool in tin completely before slicing.
steps.detail: Rest in the tin on a rack till FULLY cool — 2 hours minimum. Lift out with the paper, then refrigerate an hour for clean slicing (optional but transformative). Hot knife dipped in water between cuts gives clean edges. Store in an airtight tin up to 4 days, though they never last.

title: Sticky Pork Ribs
description: Low-and-slow ribs with a glossy glaze. Summer backyard mandatory.

steps.content: Rub ribs with paprika, sugar, salt.
steps.detail: Remove the silver membrane from the back of the ribs — peel an edge with a knife, grip with paper towel (slippery!), and pull it off in one piece. Tough if left on. Mix 2 tbsp paprika, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tbsp salt, 1 tbsp pepper. Rub hard into both sides of the ribs — press it in like you mean it.

steps.content: Wrap in foil, bake 2 hr at 150°C.
steps.detail: Lay ribs on a big sheet of foil, wrap tightly into a parcel so steam can't escape. 150°C oven, middle rack, 2 HOURS — patience is the secret. The low-and-slow breaks down the tough collagen into gelatin; you can't rush it. Ribs are ready when the meat has shrunk back from the bones and pokes give easily.

steps.content: Mix honey, soy, garlic, balsamic, sriracha for glaze.
steps.detail: Small saucepan: 1/3 cup honey, 1/3 cup soy, 4 grated garlic cloves, 2 tbsp balsamic, 1 tbsp sriracha. Bring to a bare simmer for 3 min till slightly thickened and glossy. Taste — salty, sticky, hot. Adjust. Off heat.

steps.content: Brush ribs with glaze, grill 10 min till sticky.
steps.detail: Open the foil, drain off the rendered juices (save for soup or gravy). Brush the ribs heavily with glaze. Under the grill on high, 10 min total, turning every 2 min and brushing with more glaze each time, till the surface is sticky-lacquered and lightly charred. Watch closely — sugar burns fast.

steps.content: Rest 5 min, slice between bones.
steps.detail: Rest on a board 5 min — juices settle back into the meat. Slice between each rib with a sharp knife — should glide through. Plate messy, lots of napkins. Slaw and corn on the side. Beers cold.

title: Beetroot, Goat Cheese & Walnut Salad
description: Earthy roast beetroot, tangy ricotta, toasty walnuts. Entree energy.

steps.content: Roast whole beetroot in foil 40 min at 200°C.
steps.detail: Scrub beetroot (don't peel — skin slips off easily after cooking). Trim stems leaving 2cm (stops bleeding). Wrap each in foil with a splash of oil and salt. 200°C oven, 40 min till a knife slides into the centre easily. Roasting concentrates sweetness and keeps colour; boiling leeches it.

steps.content: Peel and wedge when cool enough to handle.
steps.detail: Once cool enough to handle (but still warm), rub the skins off with paper towel — they slip off like a jacket. Wear an apron or gloves; beetroot stains everything permanently. Cut into wedges or slices, whichever looks better on the plate.

steps.content: Toast walnuts dry 3 min.
steps.detail: Dry pan over medium, walnuts in, no oil. Toss every 30 sec. 3 min till fragrant and you can hear a gentle sizzle — the oil inside is heating. Don't walk away; they burn fast. Tip onto a plate immediately (residual pan heat keeps toasting).

steps.content: Whisk oil, honey, balsamic, orange zest for dressing.
steps.detail: Small jar: 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp balsamic, 1 tsp honey, zest of one orange, pinch of salt. Shake till creamy-emulsified. The orange zest is the secret — its oils perfume everything without the juice making it runny. Taste — should be sweet, sharp, balanced.

steps.content: Toss rocket, beetroot, walnuts, dot with ricotta.
steps.detail: Big platter: rocket spread in a layer, beetroot wedges tumbled across, walnuts scattered. Pour over the dressing from height — gentle rain, not flood. Dot ricotta around in spoonfuls (don't spread — leave rough pillows). Crack of pepper. Eat before the rocket wilts from the dressing.

title: One-Pan Chorizo Pasta
description: Smoky chorizo, tomato, a hit of chilli. One pan, twelve minutes.

steps.content: Slice chorizo, render in oil till orange oil runs.
steps.detail: Slice chorizo into 5mm coins (not too thin — they need to render, not crisp to nothing). Wide pan, cold, splash of oil. Chorizo in, turn heat to medium. As the fat renders, the oil turns deep orange — that's the paprika. 4 min till the coins are crisp at the edges and the pan is painted orange. That oil IS the sauce.

steps.content: Add garlic and chilli.
steps.detail: Drop heat slightly. Three grated garlic cloves and one finely chopped chilli in. 60 sec till fragrant — don't brown. Garlic burns fast in already-hot oil.

steps.content: Tip in tomatoes, simmer 3 min.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes, crushing whole ones with your hand. Stir, scraping any chorizo fond off the bottom. 3 min simmer to let the raw tomato edge cook off and the sauce start to thicken.

steps.content: Add pasta and 400 ml water, cover, cook 10 min.
steps.detail: Add 250g pasta straight in dry (penne or spiral works best) with 400ml water. Stir, lid on. 10 min on a medium bubble. Stir every 2 min so pasta doesn't stick. The pasta cooks in the sauce, releasing starch into it — one-pan magic.

steps.content: Uncover, toss till glossy.
steps.detail: Lid off, crank heat up. Stir vigorously for 90 sec — the remaining liquid reduces and the starch thickens into a glossy sauce that coats each piece. Looks dry? Splash of water. Looks wet? Toss another 30 sec.

steps.content: Parsley and parmesan to finish.
steps.detail: Torn parsley and grated parmesan on top, plenty of black pepper. Straight into bowls. A drizzle of good olive oil at the table if you're fancy. Crusty bread for mopping.

title: Creamy Fish Pie
description: Flaked fish, peas, mash-topped. Nan's Sunday dinner distilled.

steps.content: Boil and mash potatoes with butter and splash of milk.
steps.detail: Peel and chunk 1kg potatoes. Into a pot of cold salted water (starting cold cooks them evenly). Bring to a boil, simmer 18 min till a fork slides through easily. Drain and let steam rise off for 2 min — dry potato mashes fluffier. Mash with 40g butter and a splash of milk till silky. Salt, pepper.

steps.content: Poach fish in milk 4 min, lift out.
steps.detail: Warm 500ml milk in a pan with a bay leaf and a few peppercorns if you have them — gently infuses flavour. Lay fish fillets in, bring to a bare simmer. 4 min and they're just cooked — flesh will flake. Lift out with a slotted spoon onto a plate. DON'T throw out the milk; that's your sauce base.

steps.content: Make roux with flour and butter, whisk in poaching milk.
steps.detail: Same pan: melt 40g butter, whisk in 3 tbsp flour. Cook 90 sec stirring till nutty and blonde — raw flour tastes chalky. Slowly pour in the poaching milk, whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Bring to a bare simmer stirring till thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon, 3 min.

steps.content: Fold fish, peas, parsley into sauce.
steps.detail: Off heat. Break fish into big flakes with your fingers — you want chunks, not mush. Fold into the sauce with a cup of frozen peas (no need to thaw) and a handful of chopped parsley. Taste for salt. Pepper generously.

steps.content: Pile in dish, top with mash, scatter cheddar.
steps.detail: Tip the fish mix into a baking dish — should come three-quarters up the sides. Dollop mash over the top in big spoonfuls, then spread to cover with a fork. Make furrows with the fork across the top — more surface area = more crispy golden peaks. Scatter grated cheddar across.

steps.content: Bake 20 min at 200°C till golden.
steps.detail: 200°C oven, middle rack, 20 min till the top is deep gold and the filling is bubbling at the edges. If it's pale, flash under the grill 2 min. Rest 5 min — filling is lava straight out. Peas or green salad on the side.

title: Eggs Benedict
description: The brunch order that announces you're serious. Poached eggs, ham, hollandaise, muffin.

steps.content: Separate 2 eggs; whisk yolks with lemon and dijon.
steps.detail: Separate two eggs — crack on a flat surface, gently pour the yolk between the two shell halves letting the white fall into a bowl, yolks into a heatproof bowl. Whisk yolks with 1 tsp dijon and a squeeze of lemon juice till pale and slightly thickened, 1 min. The dijon helps stabilise the emulsion.

steps.content: Stream in melted butter, whisking, for hollandaise.
steps.detail: Melt 120g butter in a small pan till fully liquid and foaming. Set the yolk bowl over a pan of barely-simmering water (don't let it touch the water — heats too fast). Whisking CONSTANTLY, stream in the hot butter in a slow drizzle. Goes from thin to thick in 2 min. If it splits: a splash of hot water and vigorous whisking usually saves it. Salt, pepper. Set over a pot of warm water off the heat to keep loose.

steps.content: Toast split muffins, warm ham briefly.
steps.detail: Split muffins with a fork (tearing gives more craggy surface than cutting). Toast till deep gold. Warm ham slices in a dry pan 30 sec a side — cold meat ruins a hot dish.

steps.content: Poach remaining eggs in simmering vinegar water, 3 min.
steps.detail: Saucepan with 10cm of water and a splash of vinegar, bare simmer (NOT boiling). Crack each egg into a ramekin first. Stir the water to create a whirlpool, slide egg into the centre. 3 min for set whites and runny yolk. Lift out with a slotted spoon, rest on a tea towel briefly.

steps.content: Stack muffin, ham, egg, spoon hollandaise.
steps.detail: Muffin half toast-side-up, slice of warm ham folded on top, poached egg balanced on that. Spoon generous hollandaise over so it cascades down the sides — don't be stingy. That ribbon of yellow is the whole point.

steps.content: Scatter parsley.
steps.detail: Torn parsley or chives scattered over. Crack of pepper, tiny pinch of paprika for colour. Eat RIGHT AWAY — hollandaise congeals as it cools and poached eggs go sad. Breakfast worth setting an alarm for.

title: Pumpkin Soup with Crispy Sage
description: Silky and warming. The unimpeachable winter entrance dish.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic in butter.
steps.detail: Finely chop a brown onion and grate 3 garlic cloves. Melt 30g butter in a heavy pot over medium. Onion with a pinch of salt, 8 min till soft and translucent. Garlic for the last minute — any longer at the bottom of a hot pot and it burns, turning the soup bitter.

steps.content: Add cubed pumpkin, stock, simmer 25 min.
steps.detail: Cube 1kg pumpkin into 3cm chunks (skin on is fine — softens in the simmer, adds nutrition). Tip into the pot with a litre of vegetable stock. Bring to a simmer, lid ajar. 25 min till the pumpkin falls apart when prodded — don't blitz before it's fully soft or you'll get lumps.

steps.content: Fry sage leaves till crisp.
steps.detail: While the soup simmers: a small pan with a knob of butter over medium till foaming. Whole sage leaves in, 30 sec a side till vivid green and crisp. Lift onto paper towel with tongs, sprinkle salt. They shatter like chips — garnish gold.

steps.content: Blitz soup with cream till silky.
steps.detail: Off heat. Pour in 150ml cream. Stick blender straight in the pot (safest) or blender in batches (cover lid with a tea towel — hot liquid creates steam pressure). Blitz 2 min till silky and smooth, no lumps.

steps.content: Season well, top with sage, bread on the side.
steps.detail: Season well — soup always needs more salt than you think. Crack of pepper, a squeeze of lemon or splash of balsamic lifts it. Ladle into bowls, swirl of extra cream on top, three sage leaves perched, crusty bread on the side for dunking. Winter in a bowl.

title: Ginger Beef Stir-Fry
description: Tender beef, snappy veg, the wok hissing. Dinner in ten.

steps.content: Slice beef thin, toss with 1 tbsp soy.
steps.detail: Freeze beef 15 min first for easier slicing. Cut ACROSS the grain into 3mm strips. Toss in a bowl with 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp cornflour if you have it. Cornflour creates a velvet coating that keeps meat juicy. Rest 10 min while you prep everything else.

steps.content: Blanch noodles.
steps.detail: Big pot of water to a rolling boil. Egg noodles in, cook 60 sec less than packet (they'll finish in the wok). Drain, rinse briefly under cold water to stop sticking, toss with a tiny splash of sesame oil so they don't clump. Set aside.

steps.content: Blast beef in hot wok 90 sec, remove.
steps.detail: Wok smoking-hot — drop of water should vaporise on contact. 1 tbsp oil, swirl. Beef in a single layer, don't touch for 60 sec — let it sear. Then toss fast for 30 more sec till edges are deep brown, centres still pink. Out to a plate.

steps.content: Stir-fry garlic, ginger, broccoli, snow peas, bok choy.
steps.detail: Same wok, more oil. 3 grated garlic cloves and a thumb of grated ginger for 30 sec till fragrant. Then broccoli florets, snow peas, halved bok choy — toss hard 2 min. Veg should stay bright and crunchy with a bit of char, not limp.

steps.content: Return beef with remaining soy and sesame oil.
steps.detail: Beef back in with resting juices. Splash 2 more tbsp soy and 1 tbsp sesame oil. Toss 30 sec till everything's glossy.

steps.content: Toss through noodles.
steps.detail: Tip the noodles in, toss another 60 sec till they're coated in sauce and everything's married. Splash of water if it looks dry. Into bowls immediately — this meal doesn't wait.

title: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes
description: Tall, cloud-soft pancakes. Weekend ritual with maple and butter.

steps.content: Whisk flour, baking powder, sugar.
steps.detail: Big bowl: 2 cups flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 3 tbsp sugar, pinch of salt. Whisk together to break up lumps and distribute the raising agent evenly. The bicarb is activated by the milk acids later — patches of it unmixed will leave bitter spots.

steps.content: Mix eggs, milk, melted butter, vanilla.
steps.detail: Separate jug: 2 eggs cracked in, 2 cups milk, 60g melted and cooled butter, 1 tsp vanilla. Beat with a fork till just combined — yolks broken up. The butter needs to be cooled or it'll scramble the eggs; pour it in last and quickly.

steps.content: Combine gently — lumps are fine.
steps.detail: Tip wet into dry and fold with a spatula till JUST combined. Lumps are GOOD. Overmixed batter develops gluten and gives you tough flat pancakes. A streak of flour still visible means you should stop.

steps.content: Rest 5 min.
steps.detail: Cover with a tea towel, rest 5 min on the bench. The flour hydrates, the gluten relaxes, the baking powder starts its lift. Skipping rest = denser pancakes.

steps.content: Cook 2 min a side in buttered pan.
steps.detail: Non-stick pan over medium (NOT high — burns the outside before the inside cooks). Swirl a small knob of butter. Ladle 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Wait till bubbles form and pop on the surface, 2 min. Flip once. 90 sec the other side. Don't press — squashes air out.

steps.content: Stack with blueberries and maple.
steps.detail: Stack five high on a warm plate. Pat of butter melting down between them, maple syrup poured generously, blueberries scattered. Eat fast; no one likes cold pancakes.

title: Eggplant & Chickpea Curry
description: Silky eggplant, warming spices, creamy coconut. A vego crowd-pleaser.

steps.content: Dice eggplant, salt 10 min, pat dry.
steps.detail: Cube two large eggplants into 3cm pieces. Salt generously in a colander, toss, rest 10 min. You'll see beads of brown liquid weep out — that's the bitterness leaving. Rinse briefly, pat DRY with paper towel. Wet eggplant won't fry; it'll stew.

steps.content: Fry eggplant in oil till golden, set aside.
steps.detail: Glug of oil in a heavy pan over medium-high. Eggplant in, don't crowd — work in two batches if needed. Each piece should touch the pan. 6 min tossing occasionally till deeply gold and soft in the middle. Eggplant absorbs oil like a sponge; you may need to add more. Set aside.

steps.content: Soften onion, garlic, ginger; add spices.
steps.detail: Same pan, drop heat to medium. Splash more oil if dry. One finely diced onion with a pinch of salt, 6 min till soft. Then 3 grated garlic cloves and a thumb of grated ginger for 60 sec. 2 tbsp curry powder and 1 tsp turmeric sprinkled in, stir 30 sec to bloom the spices — toasting wakes them up.

steps.content: Add tomato and coconut milk, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand) and a tin of coconut milk (shake first — the cream separates). Stir, scraping the bottom for any stuck spice. Bare simmer, 10 min till thickened and the raw tomato edge is gone.

steps.content: Return eggplant with chickpeas, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Eggplant back in with a drained tin of chickpeas. Stir gently — the eggplant is fragile and will turn to mush if you beat it up. 10 min simmer till everything's heated through and the flavours have married.

steps.content: Stir in spinach; serve over basmati.
steps.detail: Off heat. Two handfuls of baby spinach folded through — residual heat wilts them in 60 sec without turning them black. Taste — salt, pepper, squeeze of lime if it needs brightening. Ladle over basmati rice, torn coriander, dollop of yoghurt.

title: Seared Scallops with Pea Purée
description: Restaurant entrée at home. Caramel-crusted scallops on a vivid green purée.

steps.content: Blanch peas 2 min, plunge in iced water.
steps.detail: 1.5 cups of frozen peas into a pot of boiling salted water for EXACTLY 2 min — any longer and they lose their vivid green. Drain and plunge into a bowl of iced water to shock-stop the cooking. This preserves colour AND flavour. Drain once cold.

steps.content: Blitz peas with mint, garlic, lemon, olive oil.
steps.detail: Into a blender: the blanched peas, a big handful of mint, 1 garlic clove, juice of half a lemon, 1 tbsp olive oil, pinch of salt. Blitz 60 sec till silky — should ribbon off a spoon. Thin with a splash of warm water if stiff. Taste — should be bright, grassy, fresh.

steps.content: Pat scallops dry, season well.
steps.detail: Pat scallops REALLY dry on both sides with paper towel — wet scallops won't sear; they'll steam. Season generously with salt and pepper just before cooking (earlier and the salt draws moisture back out). Remove the small tough side-muscle if it's still attached.

steps.content: Sear 90 sec a side in hot butter.
steps.detail: Heavy pan smoking-hot. Knob of butter in, swirl. Scallops laid in with space between them. DON'T TOUCH for 90 sec — you want a deep caramelised crust. Flip once, 60 sec the other side. Inside should stay silky and translucent in the centre. Over-cooked scallops are rubbery.

steps.content: Spoon purée on plate, scallops on top, lemon wedge.
steps.detail: Big spoon of purée swooshed across each plate (back of the spoon drags a crescent). 3 scallops per person perched on top, crust-side up. Lemon wedge, crack of pepper, drizzle of your best olive oil. Restaurant entrée at home.

title: Zucchini Walnut Loaf
description: A sneaky-healthy cake pretending to be bread. Perfect with coffee.

steps.content: Grate zucchini, squeeze dry.
steps.detail: Coarse-grate zucchini into a clean tea towel. Sprinkle with a big pinch of salt, rest 5 min — salt draws out water. Wring HARD over the sink. You should pour off at least half a cup of liquid. Wet zucchini makes a gummy loaf; this step is non-negotiable.

steps.content: Whisk eggs, oil, sugar, vanilla.
steps.detail: Big bowl: 2 eggs, 1/2 cup vegetable oil, 3/4 cup sugar, 1 tsp vanilla. Whisk 60 sec till pale and slightly thickened. Pale = aerated = tender crumb.

steps.content: Fold in flour, cinnamon, baking powder.
steps.detail: Sift in 2 cups flour, 1 tsp cinnamon, 1 tsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp bicarb, pinch of salt. Fold with a spatula till just combined. Stop when a streak of flour still shows — overmixing develops gluten and makes a tough dense loaf.

steps.content: Stir through zucchini and chopped walnuts.
steps.detail: Fold in the wrung zucchini and 1/2 cup chopped walnuts. Big gentle strokes. The grated zucchini disappears as it bakes — leaves moisture and tenderness, not texture.

steps.content: Bake 55 min at 170°C in lined loaf tin.
steps.detail: Line a loaf tin with baking paper (overhang for easy lifting). Pour batter in, smooth top. 170°C oven, middle rack, 55 min. Skewer should come out with moist crumbs, not wet batter. If the top browns too fast, tent with foil the last 15 min.

steps.content: Cool 20 min before slicing.
steps.detail: Rest IN THE TIN on a rack for 20 min — the loaf is fragile hot and will break if you tip it out too early. Then lift out with the paper onto the rack to cool fully. Slices best when completely cool. Butter on a thick slice with a cuppa.

title: Duck Noodle Soup
description: Rich broth, crispy-skinned duck, rice noodles. Weeknight indulgence.

steps.content: Score duck skin, render in cold pan till crisp, 8 min.
steps.detail: Score the skin of each duck breast with a sharp knife in a criss-cross pattern — 5mm deep, don't cut into the flesh. Scoring renders fat and crisps skin. Place SKIN-SIDE DOWN in a COLD pan (no oil — duck renders its own), then turn to medium. As the pan heats, the fat slowly melts out. 8 min till the skin is shatter-crisp and mahogany.

steps.content: Flip, cook 3 min, rest.
steps.detail: Flip onto flesh side, 3 min for medium-rare. Lift onto a board, skin side up (never rests skin-down — goes soggy in its own juice). Tent loosely with foil, rest 5 min. Juices redistribute into the meat instead of pooling on the board.

steps.content: Simmer stock with ginger and garlic, 10 min.
steps.detail: Pour off most of the duck fat (save for roast potatoes — liquid gold). Into the same pan: 1.2L chicken stock, a thumb of sliced ginger, 2 smashed garlic cloves, a cinnamon stick if you have it. Bring to a simmer, 10 min. Strain into a clean pot.

steps.content: Cook noodles in broth, add bok choy last minute.
steps.detail: Rice noodles cook direct in the broth per packet, usually 4 min. Bok choy halved lengthways, add for the LAST 60 sec — you want vivid green, just-wilted. Too long and they go grey.

steps.content: Season with soy.
steps.detail: Off heat. Taste broth — season with 2 tbsp soy sauce. Might need more. Stock varies wildly; you're the judge. Some people add a teaspoon of sugar or a splash of fish sauce to round it.

steps.content: Slice duck, fan over bowls, top with spring onion and chilli.
steps.detail: Slice duck breast across the grain into 5mm slices — pink in the middle is perfect. Divide noodles and bok choy between bowls. Ladle piping hot broth over, then fan duck slices on top. Thinly sliced spring onion, fresh chilli, squeeze of lime. Serve with chopsticks and a spoon.

title: Classic Beef Burger
description: Juicy pattie, melty cheddar, lettuce, tomato, soft roll. Friday night staple.

steps.content: Shape mince into 4 fat patties, season.
steps.detail: Season mince with salt and pepper AFTER shaping — salting earlier draws out moisture and makes patties dense. Divide 600g into 4 portions. Shape into patties slightly wider than your buns (they shrink). Press a shallow dimple in the centre with your thumb — stops the patty doming up. Keep them cold till the pan is hot.

steps.content: Sear 3 min a side in hot pan.
steps.detail: Cast iron or heavy pan over medium-high till smoking. No oil — mince has enough fat. Patties in, don't touch for 3 min — you want that dark crust. Flip ONCE. Never press down with a spatula; you're squeezing out all the juice. 3 min second side for medium.

steps.content: Melt a slice of cheddar on each.
steps.detail: 30 seconds before the patty's done, lay a slice of cheddar on top of each. It'll start melting from the patty heat. Cover the pan briefly with a lid or a metal bowl for a steam boost — cheese melts perfectly, draping over the edges.

steps.content: Toast rolls, spread with mayo and dijon.
steps.detail: Same pan works for the rolls. Split, cut-side-down on the pan for 30 sec till warm and lightly toasted. Spread mayo on the bottom half, dijon on the top half. Toasting keeps the bread from turning soggy from burger juice.

steps.content: Stack roll, lettuce, tomato, onion, pattie.
steps.detail: Bottom half, lettuce leaf (catches juice), slice of tomato, thin slice of red onion, pattie with melted cheese, top half. Don't overbuild — a collapsing burger is sad. Eat two-handed, lean forward over a plate. Chippies alongside.

title: Classic Lasagna
description: Sheets of pasta, rich ragù, béchamel, a pile of parmesan. A weekend project.

steps.content: Sweat diced carrot, celery, onion, garlic.
steps.detail: Small, even dice on 1 carrot, 2 celery stalks, 1 onion, 3 grated garlic cloves. This is soffritto — the Italian flavour foundation. Heat a glug of oil in a heavy pot over medium. Veg in with a pinch of salt, 10 min till onion is translucent and carrot is softening. Don't brown — gentle sweetness is the goal.

steps.content: Brown mince, add tomatoes, simmer 30 min.
steps.detail: Crumble 700g mince in and don't touch for 3 min — let a deep brown crust form. Break up with a wooden spoon, cook 6 min more till no pink remains. Two tins of tomatoes (crush with hand), big pinch of salt, pepper, bay leaf. Simmer gently 30 min stirring occasionally — should reduce to a thick ragu that coats a spoon. A splash of milk at the end rounds the acid.

steps.content: Melt butter, whisk in flour and milk for béchamel.
steps.detail: Separate pan: melt 60g butter. Whisk in 60g flour, cook 2 min stirring — raw flour tastes chalky. Slowly pour in 750ml warm milk, whisking constantly. No lumps. Bring to a simmer stirring till thick enough to coat the back of a spoon, 4 min. Season with salt, pepper, grating of nutmeg.

steps.content: Layer pasta, ragù, béchamel, cheese in a dish.
steps.detail: Rectangular baking dish. Thin layer of ragu on the base — stops the pasta sticking. Pasta sheets across (dry sheets are fine — no need to pre-cook). Ladle ragu over, then béchamel, then grated parmesan. This is one layer.

steps.content: Repeat until everything is used, finishing with cheese.
steps.detail: Repeat: pasta, ragu, béchamel, cheese. Usually 3–4 layers. FINISH with béchamel and cheese on top — that's what gets the gold crust. Tear mozzarella chunks over the top layer for those molten pools.

steps.content: Bake 45 min at 180°C, rest 15 min before cutting.
steps.detail: 180°C oven, middle rack, 45 min till the top is deep gold and the edges are bubbling visibly. Cover loosely with foil the first 25 if it's browning too fast. REST 15 min before cutting — this is essential. Cut too early and the layers slide off each other. 15 min lets it set up into clean slices. Green salad on the side.

title: Provençal Ratatouille
description: Ribbons of summer vegetables baked in tomato. Looks like a painting, eats like heaven.

steps.content: Slice all veg into 5mm rounds.
steps.detail: Sharp knife or mandoline: slice eggplant, zucchini, and roma tomatoes into uniform 5mm rounds. Uniformity matters for both cooking AND the spiral presentation. Slice slower than feels necessary; wobbly cuts = wobbly ratatouille. Keep each veg in its own pile.

steps.content: Soften onion and garlic in olive oil, stir through tomato to make sauce.
steps.detail: Finely dice one onion and mince 3 garlic cloves. Glug of olive oil in a wide oven-safe pan over medium. Onion with a pinch of salt, 6 min till soft. Garlic last minute. Tip in 1 tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand), simmer 10 min till a loose sauce forms. Season well.

steps.content: Spread sauce in a round dish.
steps.detail: Pour the tomato sauce into a round ceramic dish (or keep using the oven-safe pan). Spread evenly across the base — this layer is the gravy the veg nestles into as it cooks.

steps.content: Fan veg slices alternating colours, in a spiral.
steps.detail: Arrange slices vertically (leaning at 45 degrees) in a tight spiral starting from the outside, alternating colours: eggplant, zucchini, tomato, capsicum ring, repeat. It should look like overlapping scales. Work inward toward the centre. Tight packing is better than loose — veg shrinks as it cooks.

steps.content: Cover with baking paper, bake 45 min at 190°C.
steps.detail: Drizzle generously with olive oil, salt, pepper, torn thyme or fresh herbs if you have them. Cover with a round of baking paper cut to fit (cartouche) — holds moisture in so veg steams-roasts instead of drying out. 190°C oven, middle rack, 45 min.

steps.content: Basil and a drizzle of oil to serve.
steps.detail: Remove paper, torn basil over the top, another drizzle of your best olive oil. Eats warm or at room temp — actually better the next day when flavours have melded. Crusty bread alongside to mop up the juices. With grilled lamb, fish, or a piece of goat cheese on toast.

title: Gnocchi al Pomodoro
description: Pillowy potato dumplings in the simplest tomato sauce. Twenty minutes.

steps.content: Warm oil with smashed garlic cloves, 2 min.
steps.detail: Smash 3 garlic cloves with the flat of a knife — don't chop. Whole garlic infuses slower and you can lift it out later. Cold pan with a good glug of oil and the garlic, turn heat to medium. Gentle sizzle, 2 min till fragrant and pale gold. DON'T let garlic go brown — bitter.

steps.content: Add tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush whole ones with your hand for rustic texture, or blitz first for smooth). A big pinch of salt, pinch of sugar if the tomatoes taste sharp. Bare simmer 10 min till thick enough to coat a spoon and the raw tomato edge is gone. Fish out garlic if it's gone dark.

steps.content: Cook gnocchi — they float when done, 2 min.
steps.detail: Big pot of heavily salted water, rolling boil. Tip gnocchi in gently. They'll sink first, then bob to the surface as they cook — that's the universal signal for done, 2–3 min total. Lift out with a slotted spoon STRAIGHT into the sauce (a little pasta water clinging is good).

steps.content: Toss through sauce with butter.
steps.detail: Add a knob of butter to the sauce with the gnocchi. Toss gently 60 sec till everything is glossy and coated. A splash of pasta water loosens if it's tight. Butter rounds off the tomato's acidity and makes everything silky.

steps.content: Basil and parmesan to finish.
steps.detail: Tear basil leaves directly over the top — don't chop, bruising turns basil black. Generous shower of grated parmesan. Crack of pepper, drizzle of your best olive oil. Eat immediately; gnocchi gets heavy once it sits.

title: Bibimbap
description: Seoul in a bowl. Vegetables, beef, rice, a runny yolk, stir it all together.

steps.content: Cook rice, keep warm.
steps.detail: Rinse 1.5 cups of rice under cold water till the water runs clear — washes surface starch off for fluffier grains. Pot with equal rice to water (Korean style, slightly less water than other rice), lid on, bring to boil, drop to lowest. 15 min. Off heat, still covered, 10 min. Fluff. Keep warm for plating.

steps.content: Stir-fry beef with soy and sesame oil.
steps.detail: Hot pan, splash of sesame oil. 300g mince in, break up and brown 5 min. Pour over 2 tbsp soy and 1 tbsp more sesame oil, 1 tsp sugar. Toss 30 sec till glossy and slightly sticky. Optional: grated garlic or ginger for depth. Set aside.

steps.content: Sauté julienned carrot, wilt spinach, pan-fry mushrooms separately.
steps.detail: Each vegetable gets its own moment. Julienned carrot — 2 min in sesame oil with a pinch of salt till just softened. Spinach — drop into salted boiling water 30 sec, shock in iced water, squeeze dry, dress with sesame oil and soy. Sliced mushrooms — hot pan till deep gold, 4 min. Keep them separate for plating.

steps.content: Fry eggs sunny-side up.
steps.detail: Non-stick pan, butter, low-medium heat. Crack eggs, don't move till whites are set and yolks still runny, 3 min. The runny yolk mixed through at the end is the traditional final flourish.

steps.content: Arrange rice in bowls, pile veg zones around, beef, kimchi, egg on top.
steps.detail: Hot rice base in a bowl. Arrange veg in zones like a pie chart — carrot, spinach, mushrooms, kimchi, beef each in their own wedge. Egg goes right in the centre. This arrangement is the visual signature of bibimbap; don't mix yet.

steps.content: Gochujang on the side, stir before eating.
steps.detail: Gochujang (Korean chilli paste) in a small bowl on the side — start with a teaspoon, add more if you like heat. Extra sesame oil in another. At the table, the eater mixes EVERYTHING together with chopsticks till it's a rough jumble — that's the whole philosophy. Literally means 'mixed rice'.

title: Beef Pho
description: Clear aromatic broth, thin rice noodles, pink beef cooked by the hot broth itself.

steps.content: Char halved onion and ginger directly on the gas, 5 min.
steps.detail: Halve a brown onion and slice a thumb of ginger into coins. Char them directly on a gas flame (use tongs) for 5 min till blackened in spots — this toasting draws out sweet smoky flavour that's essential to pho. No gas? Blast under a hot grill or in a dry hot pan till well-blackened.

steps.content: Simmer stock with onion, ginger, cinnamon, fish sauce, 25 min.
steps.detail: Into a large pot: the charred onion and ginger (skins and all — they're flavour), 1.5L beef stock, a cinnamon stick, 2 star anise if you have them, 2 tbsp fish sauce. Bare simmer, lid slightly ajar, 25 min. The broth should taste deep, aromatic, subtly sweet-spicy.

steps.content: Strain broth, keep piping hot.
steps.detail: Strain the broth through a fine sieve into a clean pot, discarding the aromatics. Keep piping hot — it needs to be near-boiling when it hits the beef or the meat won't cook properly.

steps.content: Cook noodles, divide between bowls.
steps.detail: Cook rice noodles per packet (usually 4 min in hot water till pliable with a tiny bite). Divide between 2 deep bowls. The noodles should fill about half the bowl.

steps.content: Slice beef paper-thin, lay over noodles.
steps.detail: Slice beef against the grain as thin as you can manage — ideally see-through thin. Freezing for 15 min first makes this easier. Arrange slices over the noodles in a fan pattern. They'll still be raw at this stage.

steps.content: Pour boiling broth over; beef cooks in seconds.
steps.detail: Ladle the near-boiling broth over the beef in a generous pour — should cover everything. The pouring broth cooks the paper-thin beef in seconds from pink to just-cooked. This technique is the whole magic of pho.

steps.content: Top with herbs, chilli, lime wedge.
steps.detail: Plate of herbs at the table: torn Thai basil (or regular), coriander, bean sprouts, sliced chilli, lime wedges. Each person adds to their own bowl to taste. A splash of hoisin or sriracha for the sauce, but purists frown. Eat with both chopsticks and a spoon.

title: Miso Ramen
description: Funky, savoury broth; springy noodles; a soft egg. Umami bomb in a bowl.

steps.content: Soft-boil eggs 6 min, plunge in iced water.
steps.detail: Lower eggs gently into simmering water on a spoon. Timer for EXACTLY 6 min — you want set white, jammy yolk. Straight into iced water to stop the cook and loosen the shell. Peel under a gentle stream (the steam-lift from cold shell makes it come off cleanly). Halve lengthways just before serving.

steps.content: Sear pork belly till crisp, slice.
steps.detail: Score the skin of pork belly in a criss-cross pattern with a sharp knife. Cold pan, fat-side down, medium heat — render and crisp for 8 min till the fat is mostly melted out and skin is shatter-crisp. Flip, 3 min flesh side. Rest 5 min, then slice into 5mm thick strips.

steps.content: Simmer stock with garlic and ginger, 10 min.
steps.detail: Large pot: 1L chicken stock, 2 smashed garlic cloves, a thumb of sliced ginger. Bare simmer 10 min. This infuses the stock without making it fussy.

steps.content: Whisk miso into broth, don't boil.
steps.detail: OFF HEAT (critical). Whisk 4 tbsp miso into a ladle of broth in a bowl first till smooth, then stir back into the pot. Never add miso to boiling stock — high heat kills the live cultures and makes it grainy. Taste — should be deeply savoury. Add more miso if it's flat.

steps.content: Cook noodles separately, divide between bowls.
steps.detail: Cook egg noodles separately per packet (usually 3 min) — don't cook in the broth or they turn it cloudy. Drain, divide between deep bowls.

steps.content: Ladle broth over noodles.
steps.detail: Ladle the hot miso broth over the noodles, generous amount — should come halfway up the bowl. The noodles want to sit in it.

steps.content: Top with pork, halved egg, wilted bok choy, nori, spring onion.
steps.detail: Top each bowl in a careful arrangement (ramen is as much visual as edible): slices of pork belly fanned on one side, jammy egg halved yolk-up, bok choy wilted briefly in the broth on another side, a sheet of nori leaning against the edge, sliced spring onion scattered. Sesame oil drizzle. Eat immediately.

title: Chicken Katsu Curry
description: Crisp panko chicken over rice, silky curry sauce. Japanese comfort food.

steps.content: Flatten chicken, flour-egg-crumb.
steps.detail: Butterfly chicken breasts (slice horizontally to open like a book, don't cut all the way through) and bash between baking paper till 1cm thick. Even thickness = even cooking. Flour-egg-panko (Japanese breadcrumbs — airier than regular, more crunch). Press firmly into crumbs on both sides.

steps.content: Soften onion and carrot, add curry powder.
steps.detail: Separate pan, medium heat, glug of oil. One finely diced onion and one finely diced carrot, 6 min till soft. Add 2 tbsp curry powder and stir 30 sec — blooming the spices in oil releases their flavour. If it's sticking, splash of water.

steps.content: Stir in stock, soy, honey, simmer 10 min till thick.
steps.detail: Pour in 500ml chicken stock, 1 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp honey. Bring to a bare simmer, 10 min till thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon. For silkier sauce, blitz with a stick blender — or leave rustic. Taste for salt.

steps.content: Shallow-fry chicken 4 min a side till deep gold.
steps.detail: Heat 1cm of neutral oil in a wide pan to 180°C (panko cube sizzles and turns gold in 20 sec). Crumbed chicken in, 4 min first side WITHOUT moving — you want a deep gold crust. Flip once, 3 min. Drain on a rack (not paper towel — that steams the underside soft).

steps.content: Slice, serve over rice, pour curry sauce over.
steps.detail: Slice the chicken into 2cm strips with a sharp knife — the crunch is part of the drama. Arrange over hot rice, pour the curry sauce over one end so some crumb stays crispy. Pickled red cabbage or shredded lettuce on the side cuts through the richness.

title: Pan-Fried Gyoza
description: Crispy bottoms, juicy pork, tangy dipping sauce. Fold a dozen, friends arrive.

steps.content: Salt shredded cabbage, 10 min, squeeze dry.
steps.detail: Shred 2 cups of cabbage very finely — food processor or sharp knife. Tip into a colander, sprinkle generously with salt, toss. Rest 10 min. Squeeze HARD in a clean tea towel till you've extracted the water. You should pour off half a cup or more. Wet cabbage = wet filling = soggy gyoza.

steps.content: Mix with pork, grated ginger, garlic, soy, sesame oil, spring onion.
steps.detail: Big bowl: 400g pork mince, the squeezed cabbage, a thumb of grated ginger, 3 grated garlic cloves, 3 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 2 finely sliced spring onions. Mix with chopsticks in ONE direction for 60 sec till it tightens up and becomes sticky — that binding is what keeps the filling juicy.

steps.content: Place spoonful on wrapper, pleat and seal.
steps.detail: Wrapper flat on your palm. Heaped teaspoon of filling in the centre — less is more; overstuffed wrappers split. Wet the top half of the wrapper edge with a finger dipped in water. Fold into a half-moon, then pleat the front edge (5–6 pleats) against the flat back edge to seal. Set pleated-side-up on a floured tray.

steps.content: Fry flat-side down in oil till bottoms are gold, 2 min.
steps.detail: Wide pan with a lid, 2 tbsp oil, medium heat. Line up gyoza flat-side down (not pleated side). Don't touch for 2 min — bases turn deep gold. This is the signature crispy bottom.

steps.content: Add 1/4 cup water, cover, steam 4 min.
steps.detail: Pour in 1/4 cup water (it'll hiss violently), SLAM the lid on fast to trap the steam. 4 min on medium. The gyoza steam-cooks on top while the bases stay crisp underneath.

steps.content: Uncover, crisp bases again, 2 min.
steps.detail: Lid off, crank heat up. Any remaining water evaporates and the bases crisp up again for 2 min. Listen — once you hear only sizzling (not boiling), they're done.

steps.content: Serve with soy and vinegar.
steps.detail: Slide onto a plate pleated-side up so the crispy bases face you. Dipping sauce: equal parts soy and rice vinegar (or balsamic at a pinch) with a few drops of sesame oil and chilli oil. Eat with chopsticks while hot — the crispy base softens as they sit.

title: Chicken Fajitas
description: Sizzling peppers and chicken, warm tortillas, let everyone build their own.

steps.content: Slice chicken and veg into fajita strips.
steps.detail: Slice chicken thighs across the grain into 1cm strips, 7cm long — fajita-style. Slice capsicums and onion into matching long strips, same thickness. Uniform size means everything cooks at the same speed and looks proper piled in a tortilla.

steps.content: Toss chicken with spices, lime juice.
steps.detail: Big bowl: chicken strips, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, salt, pepper, the juice of a lime. Toss till every strip is coated. 10 min while you prep the veg — the acid starts tenderising the meat.

steps.content: Blast in very hot pan 5 min, remove.
steps.detail: Biggest pan you own, smoking-hot. Splash of oil. Chicken in a single layer, don't touch for 2 min for sear. Then toss fast till cooked through, 3 more min. Crowding steams instead of sears — work in two batches if needed. Lift out with tongs.

steps.content: Char peppers and onion 4 min.
steps.detail: Same blazing pan, more oil. Capsicum strips and onion wedges in, don't touch for 90 sec for char marks. Then toss 2 min more — you want some char patches with crunch, not limp veg. Fajita means 'strips', and that smoky char is the whole point.

steps.content: Return chicken, toss.
steps.detail: Chicken back in with any resting juices, toss 30 sec to warm through and let the flavours marry. Squeeze of more lime. Serve STRAIGHT from the sizzling pan — it's the classic presentation, and it keeps the heat in.

steps.content: Serve with tortillas, sour cream, avocado, coriander.
steps.detail: Warm tortillas 15 sec a side on a dry pan. Let everyone build their own at the table: tortilla, chicken and veg, sliced avocado, sour cream, torn coriander, hot sauce. Extra lime wedges. Build, roll, eat, repeat.

title: Cheesy Quesadillas
description: Molten cheese, charred tortilla, lazy-dinner glory. Fifteen minutes top to bottom.

steps.content: Finely slice spring onion and capsicum.
steps.detail: Slice 2 spring onions thinly (whites and greens) and cut one red capsicum into small 5mm dice. Small dice matters — large chunks pop out when you fold and bite. Have cheese pre-grated (sharp cheddar + mozzarella blend melts best).

steps.content: Scatter cheese + veg over half a tortilla, fold.
steps.detail: Lay a tortilla flat. Scatter a generous handful of grated cheese over one half, then the capsicum and spring onion on top, then another handful of cheese (cheese both sides = glue that holds everything together). Fold the empty half over — half-moon shape.

steps.content: Cook in a dry pan till gold and melted, flip, 2 min a side.
steps.detail: Dry pan (no oil — the cheese creates its own seal), medium heat. Quesadilla in. 2 min first side till deep gold and you can hear cheese sizzling against the pan. Press gently with a spatula to help the bonding. Flip carefully with a wide spatula — 2 min the other side. Cheese should be visibly bubbling out the sides.

steps.content: Slice into wedges, top with coriander.
steps.detail: Slide onto a board, rest 60 sec (molten cheese burns tongues). Sharp knife, slice into wedges like a pizza. Tear coriander over.

steps.content: Sour cream for dipping.
steps.detail: Small bowl of sour cream for dipping — a splash of hot sauce stirred through is a good move. Guacamole or salsa alongside if you've got them. Eat hot, otherwise the cheese sets into a solid brick.

title: Palak Paneer
description: Vivid spinach sauce, golden cubes of paneer, warm naan. Indian date-night hero.

steps.content: Blanch spinach 1 min, blitz to a paste.
steps.detail: Big pot of water to a rolling boil, salted. 400g spinach in for EXACTLY 60 sec — you want vivid green, wilted not stewed. Drain straight into a bowl of iced water to shock-stop the cooking. Squeeze dry in a tea towel (the water is bitter). Blitz with 2 tbsp water to a rough paste. This technique keeps colour and flavour.

steps.content: Cube and pan-fry paneer till gold, set aside.
steps.detail: Cube 250g paneer into 2cm pieces. Pat very dry — wet paneer won't crust. Hot pan, splash of oil or ghee, medium-high. Cubes in a single layer, don't touch for 90 sec per side till deep gold all around. Remove to a plate. Paneer doesn't melt so you can really commit to the sear.

steps.content: Soften onion, garlic, ginger with spices.
steps.detail: Same pan. Drop heat to medium. Splash of oil. One finely diced onion, 3 grated garlic cloves, a thumb of grated ginger, 6 min till soft. Then 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp turmeric, pinch of chilli, cook 30 sec till fragrant — the spices bloom in the hot oil.

steps.content: Add tomato, simmer 10 min, then stir in spinach.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand). Simmer 10 min till thickened and the raw tomato edge is gone. Stir in the spinach paste. 3 min to warm through — don't cook long once spinach is in or it loses the bright green.

steps.content: Fold in cream and paneer, warm through.
steps.detail: Pour in 100ml cream. Slide paneer back in. Stir gently — paneer is delicate and will crumble. 3 min on low heat to warm everything and let flavours marry. Don't boil once cream's in — can split. Taste — salt, a squeeze of lemon if it's flat.

steps.content: Serve over basmati.
steps.detail: Fluff basmati. Ladle palak paneer into bowls, rice alongside. Warm naan (or flatbread) for scooping. A swirl of extra cream on top is optional but pretty. Torn coriander scattered.

title: Chana Masala
description: Spiced chickpeas that get better on day two. Pantry staple hero.

steps.content: Blitz onion, garlic, ginger to paste.
steps.detail: Food processor (or a sharp knife with patience): one brown onion roughly chopped, 3 garlic cloves, a thumb of ginger. Blitz to a rough paste. Not smooth — you want texture. This paste fries down into a richly flavoured base. Prep saves faff mid-cook.

steps.content: Fry paste with spices till fragrant.
steps.detail: Glug of oil in a heavy pan over medium-high. Tip in the paste and stir almost constantly for 8 min — it goes from wet to dry to deeply golden-brown. This is bhunao, the frying-down that's the backbone of Indian cooking. Don't rush it; patience here = flavour later. Then 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tbsp curry powder. Stir 60 sec.

steps.content: Add tomato, simmer 10 min.
steps.detail: Tip in a tin of tomatoes (crush with your hand). Scrape up any stuck paste — that's all flavour. Simmer 10 min till thickened and the raw tomato edge is gone. Oil may separate and pool on top — traditional sign that the base is properly cooked.

steps.content: Tip in chickpeas + liquid, simmer 15 min.
steps.detail: Tip in 2 drained-and-rinsed tins of chickpeas PLUS about 1/2 cup of their tin liquid (aquafaba — gives body to the sauce). Bare simmer, 15 min for flavours to soak into the chickpeas. Season well. A splash of water if it gets too thick.

steps.content: Mash a few chickpeas to thicken.
steps.detail: Scoop out about 1/4 cup of the chickpeas, mash them with a fork, and stir back into the pot. This thickens the sauce and binds it without a cornflour slurry. Old Indian grandma trick.

steps.content: Serve over rice with coriander.
steps.detail: Taste — should be deeply savoury with a citrusy edge. Squeeze of lemon if it's flat. Ladle over basmati rice, torn coriander, optional dollop of yoghurt to cool. Flatbread alongside. Leftovers are even better day two — the flavours keep developing.

title: Quick Chicken Biryani
description: Saffron-stained rice, marinated chicken, caramelised onion. A celebration in a pot.

steps.content: Marinate chicken in yoghurt + spices, 15 min.
steps.detail: Cube chicken thighs into 3cm pieces. Big bowl with 1 cup yoghurt, 2 tbsp curry powder, 1 tsp turmeric, 3 grated garlic cloves, a thumb of grated ginger, big pinch of salt. Mix till coated. Minimum 15 min; overnight is transformative — yoghurt tenderises, spices penetrate.

steps.content: Fry sliced onions till deep brown, set aside.
steps.detail: Heavy-bottomed pot with a tight lid (critical for biryani). Heat 3 tbsp oil, thinly slice 2 onions. Fry over medium-high, stirring often, 15 min till DEEPLY brown (not just gold — properly caramelised). These crispy onions are the garnish and flavour both. Reserve half on paper towel.

steps.content: Brown chicken in the same pot.
steps.detail: Same pot (don't wash — residual oil flavoured with onion is good). Medium-high heat. Tip in marinated chicken with all its yoghurt. Sear 5 min — it'll sizzle and brown. A cinnamon stick, 2 bay leaves if you have them.

steps.content: Rinse rice, layer on top of chicken.
steps.detail: Rinse 2 cups of basmati till the water runs clear — washes surface starch for separate grains. Layer on top of the chicken WITHOUT stirring. This layering is what makes it biryani vs stew.

steps.content: Pour 3 cups water over, season.
steps.detail: Pour 3 cups of water (or stock) gently down the side of the pot — don't disturb the rice layer. Big pinch of salt. A scatter of half the reserved crispy onions across the rice top. The water level should just cover the rice.

steps.content: Cover tight, cook 18 min on low, rest 10.
steps.detail: Cover with a tight lid (lay a tea towel under the lid for extra seal — dum cooking). Bring to a boil, then drop to LOWEST heat for 18 min. Off heat, DO NOT LIFT THE LID for another 10 min — this rest is crucial; steam finishes the rice.

steps.content: Fluff, top with fried onion, herbs.
steps.detail: Lift the lid — the smell is the reward. Fluff gently with a fork, bringing some chicken up from underneath without mashing rice. Scatter remaining crispy onions, torn mint and coriander across the top. Lemon wedges. A bowl of yoghurt on the side with cucumber is classic raita.

title: Falafel Bowl with Tahini
description: Crunchy chickpea patties, grains, pickled veg, creamy sauce. Meal-prep friendly.

steps.content: Blitz drained chickpeas with garlic, onion, herbs, cumin, flour.
steps.detail: Food processor: 2 drained-and-rinsed tins of chickpeas, 3 garlic cloves, half a red onion roughly chopped, big handful of parsley, big handful of coriander, 2 tsp cumin, 1/4 cup flour, pinch of chilli, salt. Blitz in pulses till a rough paste — NOT smooth. Texture matters; smooth falafel is sad hummus in a ball. Fridge 15 min to firm up.

steps.content: Shape into small patties.
steps.detail: Scoop heaped tablespoons and shape into flat patties (or balls). Flat cooks faster and crisps more edges. 12–14 patties roughly. If the mix is too wet to shape, extra spoon of flour. Too dry and crumbly, a splash of water.

steps.content: Pan-fry in oil till gold, 3 min a side.
steps.detail: Wide pan, 1cm of oil, medium-high heat. When a crumb sizzles on contact, lower patties in carefully. Don't touch for 3 min — you want deep gold crust. Flip once. 3 more min. Drain on a rack (not paper — steams the undersides soft).

steps.content: Cook quinoa.
steps.detail: Rinse 1 cup quinoa in a sieve for 30 sec (washes off saponin which tastes soapy). Pot with 2 cups water, pinch salt, lid on. Boil, drop to low, 15 min. Off heat 5 min covered. Fluff.

steps.content: Whisk yoghurt with lemon, garlic for sauce.
steps.detail: Small bowl: 1/2 cup yoghurt, juice of a lemon, 1 grated garlic clove, pinch of salt, pinch of cumin. Whisk till smooth. Too thick? Splash of water. Taste — should be tangy and garlicky.

steps.content: Build bowls: quinoa, rocket, cucumber, falafel, sauce.
steps.detail: Deep bowls. Quinoa as the base, rocket on one side, cucumber batons tumbled across, 3 falafel perched on top. Generous spoon of sauce drizzled over everything. Pickled turnip or onion if you have them. Flatbread on the side — the bowl is good but the wrap version is better.

title: Chicken Shawarma Wraps
description: Spice-rubbed chicken, garlic sauce, pickled veg, all rolled tight.

steps.content: Mix half yoghurt with spices, lemon, garlic.
steps.detail: In a big bowl: half a cup of yoghurt, 2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp turmeric, 4 grated garlic cloves, juice of one lemon, olive oil, pepper, big pinch of salt. Whisk smooth. Vivid orange-yellow and fragrant — that's the shawarma marinade. The yoghurt tenderises while the spices colour and flavour.

steps.content: Marinate chicken 30 min or overnight.
steps.detail: Tip chicken thighs straight into the marinade, toss with your hands to coat every piece thoroughly. 30 min minimum; overnight is transformative — yoghurt's lactic acid gently breaks down the muscle fibres. Cover and fridge.

steps.content: Grill or pan-fry till charred, 4 min a side.
steps.detail: Hot pan, splash of oil over medium-high (or BBQ grill). Shake excess marinade off each piece, lay in pan. Don't touch for 4 min — you want deeply charred patches that mimic spinning-vertical-spit authenticity. Flip once, 4 min. 75°C internal at the thickest part. Rest 5 min.

steps.content: Slice thin.
steps.detail: On a board, slice chicken thinly across the grain — thin slices soak up sauce better and look more authentic than chunks. Pour any resting juices over the slices; don't waste them.

steps.content: Stir remaining yoghurt with garlic, lemon.
steps.detail: Small bowl: remaining half cup yoghurt, 2 grated garlic cloves, juice of half a lemon, splash of olive oil, pinch of salt. Whisk smooth. This is tangy garlic sauce, the traditional shawarma partner — cools the spicy chicken and adds brightness.

steps.content: Warm wraps, fill with chicken, salad, sauce.
steps.detail: Warm flatbreads 15 sec a side on a dry pan. Smear sauce down the middle, pile sliced chicken over, handful of shredded lettuce, diced tomato and cucumber, thinly sliced red onion, torn parsley. Roll tight, cut in half on the bias. Eat over a plate — it gets messy.

title: Classic Meat Pie
description: Puff lid, rich mince filling. A servo staple made properly.

steps.content: Brown mince, add onion and garlic.
steps.detail: Hot heavy pan, splash of oil. Crumble 700g mince in in big chunks and don't touch for 3 min — let a deep brown crust form. Then break up with a wooden spoon. Tip in one finely diced brown onion and 2 grated garlic cloves. Cook 6 min till onion is soft and mince is well-browned. Season well.

steps.content: Stir in flour, then stock, tomato, Worcestershire.
steps.detail: Sprinkle 2 tbsp flour over the mince, stir constantly for 60 sec to cook the raw flour taste out — you're making a filling-thickening roux. Pour in 300ml beef stock in a steady stream while stirring, then half a tin of tomatoes (crush with hand) and 1 tbsp Worcestershire.

steps.content: Simmer 20 min till thick, cool.
steps.detail: Bring to a simmer, drop heat low. 20 min stirring occasionally till the sauce is thick and glossy — should hold a trail when you drag a spoon through. COOL fully before filling — hot filling melts the bottom pastry and makes the pie soggy. Spread on a tray to speed cooling.

steps.content: Line pie tin with pastry, fill with mince, top with another pastry sheet.
steps.detail: Grease a pie tin. Roll out one sheet of shortcrust pastry to line, letting it overhang the edges — you'll trim later. Fill with the cold mince mixture. Lay a second sheet of pastry on top (puff pastry traditionally for a flaky lid). Press the edges together and crimp with a fork.

steps.content: Egg wash lid, cut a vent.
steps.detail: Beat an egg with a splash of milk. Brush the lid generously with egg wash — this is what gives pies their deep gold shine. Cut a small X in the centre of the lid with a sharp knife. This vents the steam; without it the pie swells and splits.

steps.content: Bake 25 min at 200°C till deep gold.
steps.detail: 200°C oven, middle rack, 25 min till the top is deep gold and you can hear the filling bubbling gently through the vent. Rest 10 min before cutting — filling is lava straight out of the oven. Tomato sauce on the side is mandatory. Mushy peas alongside if you're feeling nostalgic.

title: Beef Stroganoff
description: Tender beef, mushrooms, silky sour cream sauce, over noodles. Nan food.

steps.content: Slice beef thin, toss with salt and pepper.
steps.detail: Freeze steak 15 min first — makes thin slicing much easier. Slice ACROSS the grain into 5mm strips. Toss in a bowl with a generous pinch of salt and pepper. That's the minimum seasoning — the sauce will add more.

steps.content: Sear in very hot pan 90 sec, remove.
steps.detail: Heavy pan smoking-hot, splash of oil. Strips in a single layer, don't crowd. 90 sec without touching — let them sear to deep brown. Toss once, 30 more sec. Medium-rare at most — they'll warm through later. Crowding steams; work in 2 batches if needed. Lift out to a plate.

steps.content: Sauté mushrooms, onion, garlic in butter.
steps.detail: Same pan, knob of butter. Drop heat to medium. Halved mushrooms in, don't touch for 2 min — you want deep gold, not grey. Then one thinly sliced onion and 2 grated garlic cloves, 6 min till soft and the mushrooms are properly browned. A pinch of salt helps.

steps.content: Deglaze with stock, stir in dijon and sour cream.
steps.detail: Pour in 200ml beef stock, scrape the pan bottom for fond. Bring to a simmer. Whisk in 1 tbsp dijon and 1 cup sour cream. Drop heat low — boiling splits sour cream into a curdled mess. 3 min gentle warming till glossy and thickened.

steps.content: Return beef, warm through.
steps.detail: Beef back in with all resting juices. Fold gently through the sauce, 60 sec to warm through — don't boil or the beef overcooks to tough. Taste — salt, pepper, a squeeze of lemon if it's too rich.

steps.content: Serve over buttered noodles with parsley.
steps.detail: Butter hot cooked noodles (egg noodles traditionally) — toss with a knob of butter so they don't clump. Pile into shallow bowls. Stroganoff ladled generously over. Torn parsley on top, crack of pepper. Comfort food incarnate.

title: Cottage Pie
description: Savoury mince under a golden mash lid. A hug in a dish.

steps.content: Sweat diced carrot, celery, onion, garlic.
steps.detail: Small even dice on 2 carrots, 2 celery stalks, 1 onion, 2 grated garlic cloves. Small dice matters — you want a piece of each in every forkful. Heavy pot, splash of oil over medium. Veg with a pinch of salt, 10 min till onion is translucent and carrot is softening. This is the mirepoix, the flavour backbone.

steps.content: Brown mince, add stock and Worcestershire, simmer 15 min.
steps.detail: Crank heat up. Push veg to the sides, crumble 700g mince into the centre of the pan. Don't touch for 3 min — deep brown crust. Then break up and cook 5 min more. Stir veg back through. Pour in 400ml beef stock and 2 tbsp Worcestershire. Simmer 15 min till thickened and the meat is sitting in a rich gravy.

steps.content: Stir in peas.
steps.detail: A cup of frozen peas straight in for the last 2 min — no need to thaw. Stir through gently. Peas are colour and sweetness.

steps.content: Boil potatoes, mash with butter and milk.
steps.detail: Peel and chunk 1.2kg potatoes. Into a pot of cold salted water, bring to boil, simmer 18 min till a fork slides through. Drain, rest 2 min to dry in the pot. Mash with 60g butter and 100ml milk till silky. Salt, pepper.

steps.content: Tip mince into dish, pile mash on top, rough with a fork.
steps.detail: Tip the mince into a baking dish. Dollop mash across the top in heaps, then spread with a fork, going right to the edges (seals the filling in). Make furrows across the top with the fork — more surface = more crispy golden peaks. Grated cheese on top is optional but wonderful.

steps.content: Bake 30 min at 200°C till golden.
steps.detail: 200°C oven, middle rack, 30 min till the top is deep gold and the filling is bubbling at the edges. If it's pale, flash under the grill 2 min. Rest 5 min — filling is volcanic straight out. Peas or green beans on the side. Sunday night comfort.

title: Caprese Salad
description: Mozzarella, tomato, basil, olive oil. Summer on a plate — buy the best of everything.

steps.content: Slice tomato and mozzarella.
steps.detail: Use the best tomatoes you can find — a caprese lives or dies by them. Ripe, room-temp (cold fridge tomatoes = tasteless). Slice 1cm thick. Do the same to the fresh mozzarella. Room-temp mozzarella, always; cold cheese is bouncy, warm cheese is velvety.

steps.content: Alternate on a platter with basil leaves.
steps.detail: Large platter. Alternate slices of tomato and mozzarella overlapping slightly in a line, with big basil leaves tucked between them. Don't chop basil — whole leaves are the traditional look. This dish is about restraint and beauty; no fussing.

steps.content: Dress with oil, a few drops of balsamic, sea salt.
steps.detail: Drizzle generously with your BEST olive oil (this is the moment to use the expensive bottle). A few drops of balsamic — a little goes a long way. Flaky sea salt — the kind you can see. No pepper needed, but a crack if you must.

steps.content: Eat immediately.
steps.detail: Serve immediately — cold-from-the-fridge tomatoes weep water, dressed tomatoes lose their shape. Eat within 20 min of dressing. Crusty bread on the side to mop up the tomato-oil-balsamic pool at the bottom of the plate — that's the best bit.

title: Tomato Bruschetta
description: Charred bread, sun-ripe tomato, basil. A backdrop to wine.

steps.content: Dice tomatoes, salt lightly, drain.
steps.detail: Dice the tomatoes into small 1cm pieces — small enough to sit on top of bread without rolling off. Tip into a bowl, sprinkle lightly with salt, toss. Rest 5 min in a sieve over a bowl — the salt draws out excess water, which would make bread soggy. Drain.

steps.content: Toss with torn basil, oil, balsamic.
steps.detail: Back in the bowl: a big glug of good olive oil, a splash of balsamic, a big handful of torn basil (not chopped — bruising turns basil black). Toss gently. Taste for salt. The tomato mix should be glossy and bright. Rest 10 min for flavours to mingle.

steps.content: Grill bread both sides.
steps.detail: Thick-cut sourdough, both sides under a hot grill (or a ridged grill pan for char marks). Watch it — goes from gold to burnt fast. You want deep gold with some charred edges. Chewy, not fragile.

steps.content: Rub hot bread with garlic clove.
steps.detail: Cut a garlic clove in half. Rub the cut face of the garlic over the HOT toast — the bread acts like a microplane, grating the garlic into every nook. Hot bread is crucial; cold bread doesn't release the garlic oils. A drizzle of olive oil.

steps.content: Pile tomato on top.
steps.detail: Pile the tomato mix generously on top, letting juice soak into the bread (that's the whole point). Extra basil leaves on top, crack of pepper. Eat within 5 min before the bread goes fully soggy. Stand up, lean over a plate. Juice on the chin is a sign of a life well-lived.

title: Parsley Tabouleh
description: A salad that's actually herbs. Bright, green, and unreasonably good.

steps.content: Cover couscous in hot water, rest 10 min, fluff.
steps.detail: Half a cup of couscous in a bowl. Cover with an equal amount of hot water (or stock for extra flavour), pinch of salt, splash of olive oil. Cover with a plate. Rest 10 min. Fluff vigorously with a fork to separate every grain — lumps ruin tabouleh.

steps.content: Very finely chop parsley, mint, spring onion.
steps.detail: Tabouleh is a HERB salad that happens to have grains in it — not the other way round. 3 big handfuls of parsley, one handful of mint, 3 spring onions.
